theme.palette.title: Theme palette
theme.palette.subtitle: Visual check of tokens — light, dark, LTR, RTL.
theme.typography.story.title: Typography — variants
theme.typography.story.subtitle: Every variant, colour, weight, alignment, plus the tabular-numerals utility.
theme.typography.story.sample-long: An investor must choose: short-term performance or long-term stability. But few allocations resist inflation, liquidity, and sector concentration at once — which is why we diversify.
theme.typography.story.sample-rich: Here's a strong word and an emphasised word in a paragraph.
theme.typography.sample.body-lg: A slightly larger lead paragraph to set the scene.
theme.typography.sample.body: A standard paragraph. It describes what's happening, no more.
theme.typography.sample.label: Email address
theme.typography.sample.caption: Updated 12 min ago
theme.icon.story.title: Icon — curated set
theme.icon.story.subtitle: 30 Lucide icons (pinned 0.469.0), 5 sizes, labelled vs decorative, RTL flip whitelist.
theme.icon.story.demo-decorative: Text with a decorative icon — the icon is hidden from screen readers.
theme.icon.story.rtl-explainer: These 5 icons flip automatically in RTL via the .theme-icon-bidi class.
theme.icon.story.rtl-non-bidi: Other icons stay identical in RTL (semantic, non-directional).
theme.separator.story.title: Separator — divider
theme.separator.story.subtitle: Horizontal/vertical × solid/dashed/dotted, decorative or semantic.
theme.separator.story.semantic-explainer: When decorative=false, screen readers announce the logical break with its orientation.
theme.skeleton.story.title: Skeleton — shimmer placeholder
theme.skeleton.story.subtitle: Three shapes (rect, circle, text), N lines, composite card. Always aria-hidden — the parent carries aria-busy.
theme.skeleton.story.composite-explainer: The parent carries aria-busy and an sr-only span describes the state to screen readers.
theme.spinner.story.title: Spinner — indeterminate indicator
theme.spinner.story.subtitle: 5 sizes, default vs inverted (on a primary surface), customisable label, inline inside a button.
theme.spinner.story.custom-label-explain: When the operation is more specific than "Loading", pass a dedicated label for screen readers.
theme.kbd.story.title: Kbd — keyboard chord
theme.kbd.story.subtitle: Single key, chord, sequence, macOS symbols, two sizes, inline + help list.
theme.kbd.story.sequence-explainer: Space separator = press in order (g then i — the Vim / GitHub pattern).
theme.kbd.story.help-explainer: Typical pattern of a keyboard-shortcuts overlay ("?" in an app).
theme.badge.story.title: Badge — status chip
theme.badge.story.subtitle: 6 variants × solid/soft × 2 sizes × leading icon × count × dot-only.
theme.badge.story.count-explainer: Above 99, the count shows as "99+". For count=0 the consumer hides the badge.
theme.badge.story.dot-explainer: Dot-only carries an aria-label (announced by screen readers) since it has no visible text.
theme.avatar.story.title: Avatar — image + initials fallback
theme.avatar.story.subtitle: 5 sizes, image or initials or user-icon fallback, 4 statuses, 6 KPI palette hues, runtime swap on img onerror.
theme.avatar.story.image-explainer: 1×1 transparent PNG — just proves that src=… does NOT trigger the controller (the image loads fine).
theme.avatar.story.icon-fallback-explain: When neither src nor initials are provided, the avatar shows the Lucide user icon.
theme.avatar.story.broken-explainer: Deliberately invalid URL → the controller swaps to initials at runtime and announces via #theme-announcer.
theme.label.story.title: Label — form field label
theme.label.story.subtitle: Visible text linked to an input via htmlFor. Required state (red asterisk + sr-only “required”), optional (muted suffix), inline hint, disabled state, two sizes.
theme.input.story.title: Input — native text field
theme.input.story.subtitle: HTML5 input styled with tokens: 8 states, 3 sizes, types text/email/password/tel/url/search/number, 120 ms focus transitions, 44 px target on mobile, aria-invalid + aria-describedby wired by Field.
theme.input.story.explainer.invalid: On error, the parent Field sets aria-invalid=true and links the error message via aria-describedby. The red border is a visual signal, never the only indicator (the error text below is mandatory).
theme.input.story.explainer.autocomplete: autocomplete and inputmode tune the mobile experience: the numeric keyboard pops for phone, email keyboard for email, and password managers can suggest the right credential.
theme.input.story.explainer.densities: data-density on a parent changes the height via --density-input-height. data-size on the input itself overrides density (per-input).
theme.textarea.story.title: Textarea — multi-line field
theme.textarea.story.subtitle: Token-styled <textarea>: autosize between minRows and maxRows (textarea-autosize controller), fixed-rows fallback when autosize is off, visual states aligned with Input, internal vertical scroll past maxRows.
theme.textarea.story.section.long-value: Very long value (clamped at maxRows + inner scroll)
theme.textarea.story.explainer.autosize: The textarea-autosize controller fits the height to the content, between minRows and maxRows. Past maxRows, the area scrolls internally — the page doesn't grow further.
theme.textarea.story.explainer.fixed: Without autosize=true (or with autosize=false), the textarea keeps the height set by rows. Useful for short comment fields where the height itself hints at the expected length.
theme.textarea.story.explainer.invalid: On error, the parent Field sets aria-invalid=true and links the error message via aria-describedby. The red border is a visual signal, never the only indicator.
theme.textarea.placeholder.message: Tell us what's on your mind in a few sentences…
theme.textarea.placeholder.notes: Internal notes (visible to your team only)
theme.textarea.sample.medium:
A message of a few lines to validate the standard rendering.

It fits on two short paragraphs — the textarea grows up to maxRows then settles.
theme.textarea.sample.long-paragraph:
A deliberately long message that exceeds the maxRows limit.

This paragraph exists to verify that the textarea doesn't push the page: past the threshold, content scrolls internally and the height stays fixed.

Vertical scroll is native (the OS scrollbar appears). Horizontal scroll is disabled (content wraps).

In readonly or disabled state, the height stays but the background switches to bg-sunken and the color to text-disabled.

Axe verifies that a visible label is wired via for/id; the bare-primitive test disables the rule (Field wires the binding correctly).
theme.native-select.story.title: NativeSelect — token-styled <select>
theme.native-select.story.subtitle: Standard HTML <select> with a Lucide chevron suffix (currentColor, RTL-native). Keeps 100 % of native OS keyboard behavior (Space, arrows, type-ahead). No Stimulus.
theme.native-select.story.explainer.inva: On error, the parent Field sets aria-invalid=true and links the error message via aria-describedby. The red border is a visual signal, never the only indicator.
theme.native-select.story.explainer.optg: Native <optgroup> elements group options in the dropdown. The optgroup label is not selectable.
theme.input-group.story.title: InputGroup — Input affixes
theme.input-group.story.subtitle: Wrapper that sticks a prefix and/or a suffix onto an Input. Affix = short text, Lucide icon, or actionable button (clear, copy, show-password). Focus delegation: clicking a non-button affix focuses the input.
theme.input-group.story.explainer.button: The suffix button emits click which is captured by data-action="click->input-group#clear". The controller wipes the value, re-dispatches input/change events, and refocuses the input.
theme.field.story.title: Field — full composition (Label + control + help/error)
theme.field.story.subtitle: Wrapper that glues Label, one of the four text controls (Input · Textarea · NativeSelect · InputGroup), a help text and an error message. Auto-generates IDs and wires aria-describedby + aria-invalid + aria-required. No Stimulus controller.
theme.field.story.section.help-and-error: Help AND error together (describedby = help + error)
theme.field.story.explainer.help-and-err: When help and error coexist, aria-describedby carries both IDs (space-separated). The screen reader reads the help, then the error. The help stays useful for reminding the user of the format even in the error state.
theme.field.placeholder.message: Describe what's on your mind in a few sentences…
theme.field.help.message-tone: Be specific — the clearer the context, the quicker we reply
theme.field.help.currency: No space or symbol — digits and decimal point only
theme.field.error.email-format: The email must contain an @ and a domain. For instance alice@samurai.com.
theme.checkbox.story.title: Checkbox — boolean selection
theme.checkbox.story.subtitle: Native <input type=checkbox> styled with kit tokens. Three visual states — unchecked, checked, indeterminate. Indeterminate is exposed via a 5-line mini-controller that sets el.indeterminate at connect — without it, the state cannot be server-rendered. No other controller — Space toggle, autofill, form-data are 100 % native.
theme.checkbox.story.explainer.indetermi: data-indeterminate=true is read at mount by the checkbox-indeterminate controller, which sets el.indeterminate=true. The browser then emits aria-checked=mixed. On the first user click the browser clears indeterminate ; the controller also cleans the data-* marker so a re-mount will not resurrect it.
theme.checkbox.story.explainer.bare: Without an inline label, Checkbox renders just the input and its visual box. Used inside Field (Field renders its Label above with for=fieldHtmlId) or inside a DataTable cell (aria-label provided). The 44 px touch target then delegates to the parent context.
theme.checkbox.story.explainer.invalid: The invalid state sets aria-invalid=true on the input and a danger border on the box. The error text itself lives in Field via aria-describedby — color alone is never the signal.
theme.checkbox.sample.terms: I agree to the terms and conditions
theme.checkbox.sample.long-label: I give my explicit consent for the processing of my personal data within this professional client dossier
theme.checkbox.description.newsletter: One letter per month — unsubscribe in one click
theme.checkbox.description.experimental: Available on Pro accounts only. May crash.
theme.checkbox.field.help.gdpr: Your data is processed per our privacy policy
theme.checkbox.field.error.gdpr: GDPR consent is required to continue.
theme.checkbox.error.required-default: This box must be checked to continue.
theme.radio-group.story.title: RadioGroup — mutually exclusive selection
theme.radio-group.story.subtitle: N <input type=radio> sharing the same name=, wrapped in <fieldset><legend>. Arrow Up/Down + Left/Right navigation is 100 % browser-managed. Tab cycle in/out as a single stop. Zero Stimulus controller. Field composition deferred to a future Ring 2 RadioField primitive — the double-labelling fieldset/legend × Field/label tension resolves cleanly in a dedicated compound.
theme.radio-group.story.explainer.invali: Invalid sets aria-invalid=true on EVERY radio + a danger border on each disc. The error text lives in Field via aria-describedby. Required is also propagated on every radio.
theme.radio-group.story.explainer.orient: Vertical by default — one radio per line. Horizontal aligns on the inline-axis — useful for 2-4 short options (Std / Exp / Pickup). Beyond that, switch back to vertical to avoid crushing labels.
theme.radio-group.story.explainer.descri: When each option carries a description, the radio-card-style pattern gives more context than a single line. The description stays muted (text-tertiary) below the primary label.
theme.radio-group.story.explainer.hidden: Sr-only legend when the visual context already establishes it — a pre-existing section header for instance. The accessible name stays available to the SR via the hidden legend.
theme.radio-group.option.long-a: Annual engagement with monthly billing and cancellation at any time
theme.radio-group.option.long-b: Lifetime engagement without renewal with free-update guarantee
theme.radio-group.description.plan-enter: For groups — unlimited users, dedicated SLA
theme.switch.story.title: Switch — on/off toggle
theme.switch.story.subtitle: Native <input type=checkbox role=switch> with a thumb-on-rails visual. Ring 1's first animated component — thumb slide + bg color crossfade at motion-duration-base (180 ms) and motion-easing-standard, per 14-motion-baseline §Switch. Reduced-motion zeros the transition via the global override. 100 % native keyboard (Space toggle, Tab move), 0 controller, autofill and form-data included.
theme.switch.story.explainer.invalid: The invalid state sets aria-invalid=true on the input + a danger border on the track. The error text lives in Field via aria-describedby — color alone is never the signal. Required forces HTML5 form validation on submit.
theme.switch.story.explainer.bare: Without an inline label, Switch renders just the track + thumb. Used inside Field (Field's Label above with for=fieldHtmlId) or inside a DataTable cell (aria-label provided). The 44 px touch target then delegates to the parent context.
theme.switch.sample.long-label: Receive a weekly summary of my team's activity by email every Monday morning
theme.switch.description.notifications: Email sent for every mention, comment or assignment
theme.switch.description.beta-features: Unstable previews — may crash, disable anytime
theme.switch.description.two-factor: Recommended for admin accounts — protects against credential stuffing
theme.switch.field.help.marketing: One letter per month — unsubscribe in one click from every email
theme.toggle.story.title: Toggle — press-and-stay button
theme.toggle.story.subtitle: <button type=button aria-pressed> that retains its state. Mini-controller toggle (~10 lines) flips aria-pressed on click and dispatches a custom `toggle` event with detail.pressed. Use cases: text toolbar (bold/italic), view switcher (grid/list), filter chip. Precursor to ToggleGroup which orchestrates N Toggles with arrow navigation and mutex/multi mode.
theme.toggle.story.explainer.variants: default is subtle (transparent off, bg-selected pressed) — for light toolbars. accent is filled (transparent off, primary pressed) — for filter chips or high-emphasis segmented controls.
theme.toggle.story.explainer.icon-only: Toggle without label → aria-label is required (axe button-name rule). The visual label is the icon ; the accessible name comes from aria-label.
theme.toggle-group.story.title: ToggleGroup — orchestrate N Toggles
theme.toggle-group.story.subtitle: role=group container that orchestrates N Toggles with arrow navigation (Left/Right horizontal, Up/Down vertical), Home/End first/last, roving tabindex (only one Toggle tabbable at a time), and mutex single mode (radio-like) or multi mode (checkbox-like). The toggle-group controller listens for the `toggle` event bubbling from Toggle children.
theme.toggle-group.story.explainer.singl: Only one Toggle pressed at a time. Pressing a new one auto-unpresses the previous. Typical use — view switcher (grid/list), density (comfortable/cozy/compact), plan segmented control.
theme.toggle-group.story.explainer.multi: Each Toggle keeps its own state independently. Typical use — text format toolbar (bold + italic + underline together), filter chips where several filters apply in parallel.
theme.toggle-group.story.explainer.orien: Vertical stacks Toggles on the block-axis and switches arrow nav to Up/Down. Used for sidebar tabs or to pack multiple groups side-by-side horizontally.
theme.slider.story.title: Slider — numeric value selection
theme.slider.story.subtitle: Native <input type=range> single-thumb. 100 % native keyboard — Arrow Left/Right, Home, End, PageUp, PageDown all browser-managed. Form-data POST included. Touch drag native. Styling via vendor-prefixed pseudos ::-webkit-slider-thumb / ::-moz-range-thumb. Zero Stimulus controller. Dual-thumb, marks, range mode — Ring 2 Compound (RangeSlider, see manifest thirdPartyLibs.slider:noUiSlider).
theme.slider.story.explainer.value-displ: valueDisplay=true renders an <output for=fieldHtmlId> in the row header with the initial value + optional suffix (% / px / €). Live update on drag is consumer's job — wire data-action=input->myCtrl#update to sync output.textContent. Future Ring 2 SliderField will compose Slider + Label + valueDisplay + live update + help.
theme.slider.story.explainer.invalid: Invalid sets aria-invalid=true + danger border on the track + danger thumb. The error text lives in Field via aria-describedby. Required forces HTML5 validation on submit.
theme.slider.story.explainer.sizes: Three sizes — sm (4 px track / 14 px thumb), md (6 / 18), lg (8 / 22). md/lg recommended on touch ; sm for dense screens or inline-with-text.
theme.slider.story.explainer.range: min, max, step control the domain and granularity. Negative supported. The thumb positions at (value - min) / (max - min) of the track, browser-managed. The visible fraction is the same in LTR and RTL — input type=range respects writing-direction natively.
theme.slider.story.explainer.bare: Without an inline label, Slider renders just the input. Accessible name via aria-label (DataTable cell case) or via Field's external Label with for=fieldHtmlId. The valueDisplay becomes consumer's responsibility in bare mode.
theme.slider.label.long: Engagement level required to finalize your enrollment in the annual premium program
theme.button.story.title: Button — primary interactive surface
theme.button.story.subtitle: Native <button type=button> or <a href> styled identically. 5 variants (primary, secondary, ghost, destructive, link) × 3 sizes (32 / 40 / 48 px) × leading / trailing icon × width-stable loading × full-width × link-styled-as-button. Press-back scale(0.97) instant, release fast/decelerate (Toggle pattern). Touch ≥ 44×44 px under coarse pointer. Zero Stimulus — Enter / Space native.
theme.button.story.explainer.variants: primary for the main action; secondary for the alternative; ghost for tertiary toolbar actions; destructive for irreversible actions; link for inline prose actions.
theme.button.story.explainer.sizes: md (40 px) is the default, matching --density-button-height and Input md. sm for dense contexts (toolbars, DataTable cells). lg for hero / auth-page CTAs.
theme.button.story.explainer.loading: loading=true sets aria-busy + disabled. The spinner replaces iconLeading when present (width stable); otherwise it prepends in the leading slot. iconTrailing stays visible — it carries the semantics of where the action leads.
theme.button.story.explainer.full-width: fullWidth=true sets inline-size: 100%. Typical form-submit pattern — the button fills its enclosing Field's width.
theme.button.story.explainer.as-link: When href is passed, the render flips <button> → <a> while keeping the visual. aria-disabled replaces the HTML disabled attr on the anchor branch. Useful for navigation styled as an action — preserves right-click-open-in-new-tab.
theme.button-group.story.title: ButtonGroup — Stitched buttons (segmented action control)
theme.button-group.story.subtitle: role=group container that stitches N Buttons together with collapsed adjacent borders (segmented look). Each cell keeps its own Tab stop — this is NOT roving-tabindex (see ToggleGroup for that). Horizontal (default) or vertical. Detached variant uses a gap instead of border-collapsing. Zero Stimulus.
theme.button-group.story.explainer.defau: The most common pattern — two segmented buttons at the end of a dialog or form. Secondary on the left (Cancel), primary on the right (Confirm / Save).
theme.button-group.story.explainer.verti: orientation=vertical stacks the Buttons on the block-axis. Useful for sidebar action stacks or row detail panes in a narrow cell.
theme.button-group.story.explainer.detac: attached=false removes the border-collapsing — each Button gets its full rounding back + a spacing gap. Typical dialog footer when Cancel is ghost and Confirm is primary (ghost reads as standalone but the primary remains in the action cluster).
theme.divider.story.title: Divider — separator with center slot
theme.divider.story.subtitle: Visual separator with an optional center slot (label, icon, or both). Distinct from Separator (which is a bare line). The « ──── or ──── » auth-screen pattern or « ──── Recent articles ──── » list-section pattern. Plain mode without content is visually equivalent to Separator. CSS logic — flex with two flex-grow segments + intrinsic-width center slot. Zero Stimulus.
theme.divider.story.explainer.with-label: The center label is wrapped in a span with background-color: --color-bg-base — the effect is that the label «masks» the traversing line. For non-base backgrounds, override --color-bg-base at the Divider level via a utility class.
theme.divider.story.explainer.vertical: Vertical is useful for inline rows (« Lorem | ipsum | dolor »). The with-content vertical (« FR | or | EN ») also works but is visually rare.
theme.divider.story.explainer.semantic: decorative=false sets role=separator + aria-orientation. Use when the divider marks a semantic logical boundary (form field grouping, auth option partition). For pure decoration, keep decorative=true (default).
theme.aspect-ratio.story.title: AspectRatio — inline/block ratio wrapper
theme.aspect-ratio.story.subtitle: CSS box that maintains a defined ratio via the native aspect-ratio property (Baseline 2022). Zero polyfill. Used for cover images, video iframes, 1/1 avatars, 1.91/1 Open Graph placeholders, 9/16 Stories. object-fit modifiers (cover / contain / fill / scale-down) on the inner element. Skeleton inner = canonical loading state. No ARIA role added — the inner element carries semantics.
theme.aspect-ratio.story.explainer.commo: 16/9 is the HD video ratio; 4/3 the classic photo ratio; 1/1 for Instagram / avatars; 1.91/1 the Open Graph ratio (social link preview); 9/16 Stories portrait; 21/9 cinemascope.
theme.aspect-ratio.story.explainer.fit: cover (default) crops the image to fill the box. contain letterboxes the image so it fits entirely. fill stretches it (distorts). scale-down = min(contain, none) — small images keep their native size.
theme.aspect-ratio.story.explainer.ifram: The wrapper adapts to the AspectRatio box, so a responsive iframe (YouTube video, Mapbox map, 3D model) stays at its native ratio with no JS measurement.
theme.aspect-ratio.story.explainer.empty: AspectRatio + Skeleton inner = the kit's canonical empty/loading pattern. The parent owns aria-busy=true + an sr-only description (see Skeleton spec).
theme.card.story.title: Card — surface container
theme.card.story.subtitle: Surface container with 3 optional slots (header, body, footer) via {% embed %} Twig blocks. 3 variants: surface (default), elevated (lifted shadow), subtle (bg-base, no shadow). Hoverable opt-in (visual only — wrap in <a>/<button> to activate). Divider opt-in via --theme-card-divider: 1px. <article> semantic landmark.
theme.card.story.explainer.variants: surface (default) for common page cards; elevated for modal-adjacent / peek-out cards that need to detach; subtle for nested settings cards inside a tab — lower emphasis, flat tone.
theme.card.story.explainer.hoverable: hoverable=true paints only the perception (shadow lift + cursor pointer). No click handler attached — consumer wraps in <a> or <button> if the whole surface should activate. :focus-within paints the ring on the Card when the wrapper has focus.
theme.card.story.explainer.divider: Inline style --theme-card-divider: 1px adds a subtle border between header / body / footer + padding-block on each slot. Typical case: settings cards inside the Account tab.
theme.card.story.explainer.composition: Card composes cleanly with AspectRatio (cover-image article card) and Badge (status KPI card). The body slot is a flex column div with gap — each child breathes naturally.
theme.card.story.sample.article-body: New side navigation, real-time KPIs, and 20% load-speed improvement. Available for all Pro accounts from May 15.
theme.card.story.sample.setting-body: Choose how you want to be notified — by email, mobile push, or both. You can always change later.
theme.card.story.sample.hoverable-body: The shadow lifts on hover and the cursor switches to pointer. To make the whole surface clickable, wrap the Card in <a> or <button>.
theme.card.story.sample.long-title: Long heading to validate the wrap of long titles in English with hyphenation
theme.card.story.sample.long-body: A deliberately long paragraph to test inner wrapping and +30% expansion in FR. Includes accented characters in the FR source, digits ($12,480, 8.2%), and punctuation. The min-inline-size: 0 on the body lets the text wrap cleanly.
theme.card.story.sample.cover-body: Photo report on the disused railway line of the Petite Ceinture.
theme.item.story.title: Item — selectable list row
theme.item.story.subtitle: The unit of DropdownMenu, DataTable row, NavMenu item, ContextMenu, Combobox result (all Ring 2). Slots leading (icon / avatar / checkbox) + label + description + trailing (icon / Kbd shortcut). 3 element forms: div (parent-orchestrated), a (standalone link), button (standalone action). Visual selection via data-selected, semantic via aria-current. Zero Stimulus at primitive level.
theme.item.story.explainer.states: data-selected=true is the visual flag (highlight the current row of a Combobox). data-state=disabled mutes the row. focus-visible paints an inset outline only on interactive variants.
theme.item.story.explainer.elements: as='div' (default) — non-interactive, orchestrated by DropdownMenu/DataTable/NavMenu (Ring 2). as='a' — standalone link (native Tab + Enter). as='button' — standalone action. The CSS visual is identical across 3 — choosing the right element is a semantic decision.
theme.item.story.explainer.kbd: The trailing slot accepts a Kbd shortcut (mutually exclusive with iconTrailing). Command palette pattern — the user discovers shortcuts by browsing the menu.
theme.item.story.explainer.description: When description is set, label + description form a 2-line row (label medium, description xs muted). Search-results or detailed list-view pattern.
theme.item.story.explainer.aria-current: aria-current="page" is the W3C-blessed pattern for « you are here » in navigation. Visually identical to data-selected but semantically distinct (current page vs simple option highlight).
theme.item.story.sample.desc-search: Search across all projects and tasks
theme.item.story.sample.desc-shortcut: Quick access to your favourite items
theme.progress.story.title: Progress — task indicator
theme.progress.story.subtitle: Progress indicator with 2 modes: determinate (native <progress value max>, vendor-prefixed pseudos for styling) and indeterminate (CSS-only animated diagonal stripe, OQ-17). 4 color variants (primary/success/warning/danger). 3 sizes (4/6/8 px). Reduced-motion freezes the stripe (still indicative). Zero controller — consumer mutates progress.value programmatically.
theme.progress.story.explainer.value-dis: valueDisplay=true shows the current value in the header (tabular-nums for stable alignment). Suffix is consumer-provided — '%', ' / N', ' MB', ' €' depending on context. Live update is consumer's job — wire data-action=progress->myCtrl#tick on the emitting element.
theme.progress.story.explainer.variants: primary by default. success for 100% positive completions (export done, save succeeded). warning around 70-90% threshold (disk space, quota). danger > 90% critical (disk full, low battery).
theme.progress.story.explainer.indetermi: No value → indeterminate mode. The diagonal stripe animates via background-position keyframes (1.6s linear infinite). Reduced-motion freezes the stripe — it stays visible but motionless. OS-consistent pattern (macOS, Windows, GNOME progress bars).
theme.progress.story.explainer.bare: Without label or valueDisplay, Progress renders just the bar. Accessible name via aria-label if the context doesn't already describe it.
theme.progress.story.label.long: Synchronizing all team files with the production server
theme.tooltip.story.title: Tooltip — hover/focus descriptive popup
theme.tooltip.story.subtitle: Wrapper + role=tooltip with CSS-only visibility (hover OR focus reveal), 400 ms show delay (anti-flash), instant hide (snappy), 4 placements (top/bottom/start/end). Esc dismiss via mini-controller `tooltip` (15 lines). aria-describedby always set on the trigger — SR hears the description on focus regardless of visual state.
theme.tooltip.story.explainer.default: The most common pattern: icon-only ghost button with a tooltip revealing its action. aria-label sets the button's accessible name; aria-describedby points to the tooltip for the long description. Hover OR focus triggers the show after 400 ms.
theme.tooltip.story.explainer.placements: 4 placements via data-placement: top (default, anchored center horizontal), bottom (same), start (anchored center vertical, inline-start), end (same). In RTL, start/end flip naturally (logical CSS).
theme.tooltip.story.explainer.icon-butto: Toolbar pattern — each icon-button has its own tooltip. Focus moves between buttons (Tab); each focus triggers its tooltip 400 ms later. Esc dismisses the current tooltip; refocus re-arms it.
theme.tooltip.story.explainer.long-conte: Beyond max-inline-size 280 px, the tooltip wraps onto multiple lines. The caption typography + snug line-height keep the density.
theme.tooltip.story.explainer.esc: The `tooltip` controller (15 lines) listens for keydown.esc on the wrap + focusout. Esc stamps data-state=dismissed; focusout (outside the wrap) resets the flag. Covers the WAI-ARIA APG rule « Tooltip — Hover or focus shows; Esc dismisses ».
theme.tooltip.story.sample.long-content: This action will open the settings panel in a new tab. There, you can update your notification, security, and billing preferences. All changes are saved automatically.
theme.tooltip.story.sample.link-hint: Article published 2 days ago — estimated read time 5 min
theme.tooltip.story.sample.esc-hint: Press Esc to close; refocus to re-arm
theme.alert.story.title: Alert — inline status banner
theme.alert.story.subtitle: 4 variants (info / warning / danger / success) × 2 tones (soft / solid). Optional icon + title + body + dismiss + action. role=alert on danger (assertive), role=status otherwise (polite). Appear animation 180 ms emphasized; dismiss via lightweight controller (~12 lines) + announce via #theme-announcer.
theme.alert.story.explainer.default: A minimal Alert — variant + body. Role derives from variant: alert for danger (implicit assertive aria-live), status otherwise (implicit polite aria-live). No explicit aria-live attribute needed.
theme.alert.story.explainer.variants: Soft tones — muted bg + 1 px border + dark text. Best for persistent banners (form errors, long-running info). All *-soft-foreground × *-soft pairs clear AA in the contrast matrix.
theme.alert.story.explainer.tones: Solid tones — saturated bg + contrasting foreground (dark for warning and info per ADR-0007, light for danger and success). Higher visibility but more aggressive — reserve for critical announcements.
theme.alert.story.explainer.full-anatomy: Full anatomy: variant icon + semibold title + body paragraph + ghost action button + dismiss button (alert-dismiss controller). On close, opacity transitions out in 120 ms accelerate → DOM remove + dispatches alert:dismissed + SR announcement.
theme.alert.story.explainer.no-icon: hideIcon hides the icon. Useful when the surface (color + border) already conveys the status, or for a more minimal design.
theme.alert.story.explainer.long-content:
Body wraps naturally. Multi-paragraph via inner

elements — spacing handled by .theme-alert__body p + p (margin-block-start spacing-2). +30 % FR expansion tested.
theme.alert.story.sample.info-body: A new version of the kit is out. Refresh the page to get it.
theme.alert.story.sample.warning-body: You've used 92 % of your storage. Free up space or upgrade to the Pro plan.
theme.alert.story.sample.danger-body: Can't reach the server. Check your connection or try again in a moment.
theme.alert.story.sample.full-title: Action needed — finish setting up your account
theme.alert.story.sample.full-body-p1: To unlock every feature, confirm your email address and add your phone number.
theme.alert.story.sample.full-body-p2: It only takes a few seconds.
theme.alert.story.sample.no-icon-body: This banner relies on its background color alone as the signal. Use when the context makes the icon redundant.
theme.alert.story.sample.long-p1: Your full data export is in progress. Depending on the volume, this may take a few minutes — feel free to close this tab in the meantime.
theme.alert.story.sample.long-p2: We'll email you as soon as the archive is ready for download. Exports stay available for 7 days.
theme.alert.story.sample.long-p3: If you run into trouble, contact support from the Help menu or try the export again in a few minutes.
theme.empty.story.title: Empty — empty state / first-time
theme.empty.story.subtitle: Composition surface for "no data / no results / first-time / 404" states. Illustration + title + body + CTAs + footer. 3 sizes (sm in-card · md section · lg page-hero). Zero controller. Microcopy that invites, never blames.
theme.empty.story.explainer.default: "No results" pattern — Lucide icon (search) + title + factual body + secondary CTA "Clear filters". The title is short, the body proposes an action.
theme.empty.story.explainer.sizes: sm fits in a Card / row; md fills a page section; lg carries a custom illustration (consumer-provided inline SVG) — typical 404 and onboarding. The heading level adapts (h3 sm / h2 md / h1 lg via titleLevel).
theme.empty.story.explainer.hero: For lg, the consumer provides the illustration via {% block illustration %} (OQ-18 doctrine — Lucide for dense contexts, custom SVG for hero). The SVG here uses --color-primary-soft + --color-border-default to stay on tokens.
theme.empty.story.explainer.composition: Empty fits naturally inside a Card's body block. Card provides the surface + shadow + radius; Empty provides the centered composition. Common pattern for empty sections of a dashboard.
theme.empty.story.explainer.full-anatomy: Full anatomy: illustration + title + body + primary CTA + secondary CTA + kbd hint footer. Power-user discovery via Kbd in the footer; primary CTA in accent, secondary in outline for hierarchy.
theme.empty.story.sample.no-results-body: Nothing matches your filters. Try another term or clear the filters.
theme.empty.story.sample.no-projects-bod: Create your first project or browse the examples for inspiration.
theme.empty.story.sample.no-team-body: Invite your team to collaborate on your projects.
theme.empty.story.sample.no-notification: We'll write if anything comes up.
theme.empty.story.sample.no-favorites-bo: Star an item to find it here later.
theme.empty.story.sample.404-body: The link may be outdated, or the page has moved.
theme.empty.story.sample.long-title: No project matches your current search criteria
theme.empty.story.sample.long-body-p1: Try broadening your filters or using more general keywords. You can also browse the examples below for inspiration.
theme.empty.story.sample.long-body-p2: If you're looking for something specific that isn't here, feel free to create a new project — most templates can be customized.
theme.collapsible.story.title: Collapsible — animated disclosure
theme.collapsible.story.subtitle: Custom button aria-expanded + sibling content with CSS-only grid-template-rows 0fr→1fr animation (OQ-16 — native <details> doesn't animate without JS interception). Lightweight controller (~25 lines): toggles aria-expanded + data-state + dispatches collapsible:toggle, which bubbles up to the Accordion orchestrator.
theme.collapsible.story.explainer.defaul: FAQ pattern — question in the trigger, answer revealed on open. The chevron-down rotates 180° when opened. aria-expanded on the button + aria-controls to the content + role=region + aria-labelledby to the trigger.
theme.collapsible.story.explainer.open-b: open=true mounts the Collapsible in its open state. The controller's connect() syncs data-state from aria-expanded to avoid mount flicker.
theme.collapsible.story.explainer.no-che: iconChevron=false hides the chevron — useful when another visual signal (leading icon, parent context) already conveys the expand/collapse affordance.
theme.collapsible.story.explainer.multip: Three independent Collapsibles — each manages its own state. This is the multi mode Accordion (next primitive) offers. For single (mutex: only one open at a time), wrap in an Accordion.
theme.collapsible.story.explainer.compos: Collapsible fits naturally inside a Card. Typical pattern: Card header for the section title + Card body for the expandable detail. Settings, profile details, doc sections…
theme.collapsible.story.sample.faq-1-que: How do I add a team member?
theme.collapsible.story.sample.faq-1-ans: Head to Settings → Team → Invite. Enter the email and pick a role. The invitation expires after 7 days.
theme.collapsible.story.sample.faq-2-ans: Yes — go to Settings → Data → Export. The export is a ZIP archive containing all your projects as JSON plus the attached files. It stays available for 7 days.
theme.collapsible.story.sample.faq-3-que: How do I change my password?
theme.collapsible.story.sample.faq-3-ans: Go to Settings → Password. Enter your current password and the new one (≥ 8 characters). Every other session will be signed out.
theme.collapsible.story.sample.details-b: The server uses an LRU cache of 256 MB. Idle requests expire after 15 minutes. Exports are gzip-compressed at level 6.
theme.collapsible.story.sample.in-card-s: Email + push depending on event type.
theme.collapsible.story.sample.in-card-b: You receive an email for every direct mention and a daily push digest. To fine-tune, open the detail below.
theme.collapsible.story.sample.long-titl: Privacy policy and terms of service
theme.collapsible.story.sample.long-p1: Your personal data is stored on servers hosted in the European Union, in line with GDPR. We never share data with third parties without your explicit consent.
theme.collapsible.story.sample.long-p2: You can request a full export, a correction, or a deletion of your data at any time from Settings → Privacy. Requests are processed within 30 days.
theme.collapsible.story.sample.long-p3: Use of the service implies acceptance of our terms of service, viewable in full on our dedicated page. Major changes are notified via email with a 30-day notice.
theme.accordion.story.title: Accordion — orchestrator of N Collapsible
theme.accordion.story.subtitle: Composes N Collapsible with single mode (mutex, one open at a time) or multi mode (independent). Lightweight controller (~80 lines) with roving tabindex + Arrow Up/Down + Home/End. Enter/Space activate via each Collapsible's native button — Accordion does NOT intercept.
theme.accordion.story.explainer.single: FAQ pattern — opening one answer closes the previous one. The controller listens for the bubbled collapsible:toggle event from children and closes the others by directly mutating aria-expanded + data-state (no re-dispatch, avoids the feedback loop).
theme.accordion.story.explainer.multi: Preferences pattern — each item opens independently. Several can be open simultaneously. The controller still handles keyboard nav (Arrow Up/Down between triggers) but skips the mutex enforcement.
theme.accordion.story.explainer.disabled: A disabled trigger is skipped by Arrow nav and excluded from the roving tabindex. Still visually present (dim opacity, cursor: not-allowed) but not focusable.
theme.accordion.story.explainer.many-ite: 5 items to demo keyboard navigation. Tab enters the group → focus lands on the open item (single) or the first (multi). Arrow Up/Down cycle between triggers. Home / End jump.
theme.accordion.story.explainer.composit: Accordion fits naturally inside a Card. Typical pattern: Card for the global surface (section title + summary metadata), Accordion inside for expandable details. Settings, product FAQ, reference docs.
theme.accordion.story.sample.details-bod: This section is disabled because the user lacks the required permissions.
theme.accordion.story.sample.pref-1-body: Get a daily digest at 9 a.m. (local time) with mentions, project updates, and pending invitations.
theme.accordion.story.sample.pref-2-body: Enable push for direct mentions only. Other events stay in the app's notification area without a buzz.
theme.accordion.story.sample.pref-3-body: Browser push requires system permission. If you aren't prompted, go to your browser settings to enable it.
theme.accordion.story.sample.legal-1-bod: Using the service implies acceptance of our terms. Major changes are notified via email with a 30-day notice.
theme.accordion.story.sample.legal-2-bod: Your data stays hosted in the EU. You can request an export, correction, or deletion at any time.
theme.accordion.story.sample.legal-3-bod: We use strictly necessary cookies (session, security) and — opt-in — analytics cookies. No third-party marketing cookies.
theme.accordion.story.sample.legal-4-bod: Full refund within 14 days for annual subscriptions (consumer right of withdrawal). Pro-rata refund after.
theme.accordion.story.sample.legal-5-bod: Cancel any time from Settings → Billing. No hidden fees. Effective at the end of the current billing period.
theme.accordion.story.sample.card-summar: 3 channels — pick the ones that work for you.
theme.breadcrumb.story.title: Breadcrumb — navigation trail
theme.breadcrumb.story.subtitle: Trail of <a> separated by chevron-right (auto-flips RTL via the bidi whitelist). Last item = current page: non-link with aria-current="page". Wrapped in a <nav aria-label> landmark. Zero controller. Overflow ellipsis → Ring 2.
theme.breadcrumb.story.explainer.default: Most common pattern — 3 levels. The first two are links to parent pages; the third is the current page (non-link, aria-current=page, font-weight medium).
theme.breadcrumb.story.explainer.four-le: When depth exceeds 4-5 levels the pattern gets noisy. Ring 2 will add a variant with a middle "…" ellipsis (DropdownMenu for the hidden levels).
theme.breadcrumb.story.explainer.with-ic: A leading icon can reinforce semantics (home, settings, danger). Always decorative (aria-hidden) — the text label carries the meaning.
theme.breadcrumb.story.explainer.current: A single-item Breadcrumb (the current page) stays valid — no separator. Useful for root pages where the unified component keeps the layout consistent.
theme.popover.story.title: Popover — floating panel anchored to a trigger
theme.popover.story.subtitle: The JS-positioned counterpart to Tooltip (Ring 1 CSS-only 4 placements). Floating UI v1.7 provides viewport-aware positioning (flip + shift collision detection). Popover is non-modal: the trigger keeps focus on open; the user Tabs into the content if they want. Window-scoped Esc dismiss + click-outside dismiss (capture phase). Foundational compound: 6 downstream compounds (Combobox, DropdownMenu, ContextMenu, HoverCard, DatePicker, Select) compose Popover.
theme.popover.story.explainer.default: The most common pattern: click on a secondary Button → popover opens below with a small surface (title + body). Esc closes; click outside closes. Focus stays on the trigger after opening — Tab into the content only if you want to interact.
theme.popover.story.explainer.placements: 4 logical placements: top, bottom, start, end. 'start' / 'end' are logical aliases converted to physical 'left' / 'right' by the controller per document.documentElement.dir — auto-RTL. Floating UI's flip() switches to the opposite placement if the viewport is short on space.
theme.popover.story.explainer.with-form: The content can contain interactive elements (Input, Button). Tab from the trigger enters the content; elements are focusable; Tab continues; past the last one, focus leaves the popover. No focus trap — for that you need Dialog (modal).
theme.popover.story.explainer.collision: Place the trigger near the viewport edge: Floating UI's flip() detects the collision and switches placement (e.g. top → bottom if no room above). shift() adjusts the inline offset to stay visible.
theme.popover.story.explainer.long-conte: Beyond max-inline-size 360 px, the content wraps onto multiple lines. The min-inline-size 200 px prevents popovers from being too narrow.
theme.popover.story.explainer.events: The controller dispatches popover:open / popover:close (bubbles + composed) as raw CustomEvents. detail.placement carries the placement resolved after flip(). Consumers can listen on the wrap or higher up.
theme.popover.story.sample.tags-body: Pick one or more labels to categorize this project. Changes save automatically.
theme.popover.story.sample.placement-con: Placement top — appears above the trigger.
Placement bottom — appears below the trigger.
Placement start — appears at the inline-start (left in LTR, right in RTL).
Placement end — appears at the inline-end (right in LTR, left in RTL).
theme.popover.story.sample.collision-bod: Initially placed top. If short on space, Floating UI switches to bottom (and vice versa).
theme.popover.story.sample.long-body: This popover contains denser content that demonstrates multi-line wrapping. The max-inline-size 360 px prevents popovers from sprawling into banners; the content wraps onto as many lines as needed. Density stays readable thanks to the normal line-height and 12 px inline padding.
theme.popover.story.sample.events-body: Open and close this popover several times. The log below captures every popover:open / popover:close.
theme.dialog.story.title: Dialog — modal native `<dialog>` + ::backdrop
theme.dialog.story.subtitle: Per OQ-20 doctrine, the kit uses the native `<dialog>` element (Baseline 2022) with `showModal()`. Auto top-layer, native ::backdrop, native focus trap (Tab cycles within the dialog), native Esc dismiss. The `dialog` controller (~120 lines) handles open/close + focus return + backdrop click policy + Esc dismiss policy. CSS-only animations via `@starting-style` + `transition allow-discrete` (Baseline 2024).
theme.dialog.story.explainer.default: The most common pattern: a Button trigger opens a modal dialog with title + body + footer (Cancel + Confirm). Esc closes; backdrop click closes; the close-X in the header closes. Focus returns to the trigger on close. In LTR Cancel is left of Confirm; in RTL it's the opposite (DOM order [Cancel, Confirm] + flex-row writing direction = natural).
theme.dialog.story.explainer.sizes: 3 sizes via data-size: sm (400px, simple confirmations), md (480px, default), lg (720px, richer content — settings, multi-field).
theme.dialog.story.explainer.with-form: The body can contain an interactive form. Tab cycles WITHIN the dialog only (native focus trap via showModal). The form submit can call ctrl.close() after validation. Focus return goes to the original trigger.
theme.dialog.story.explainer.destructive: For destructive actions (permanent deletion, payment), disable Esc dismiss + backdrop click via closeOnEscape=false + closeOnBackdropClick=false. The user MUST click an explicit button. The close-X stays available for clear dismissal.
theme.dialog.story.explainer.long: When the body is larger than the viewport, the dialog scrolls (overflow: auto + max-block-size: calc(100dvh - 2*spacing-4)). The background page does not scroll (native focus trap).
theme.dialog.story.explainer.events: The controller dispatches dialog:open / dialog:close (bubbles + composed) as raw CustomEvents. Consumers listen on the wrap or document.
theme.dialog.story.sample.default-body-1: Enter the person's email address. An invitation will be sent immediately.
theme.dialog.story.sample.size-body-sm: 400 px max-inline-size. For simple confirmations — a question, two buttons.
theme.dialog.story.sample.size-body-md: 480 px max-inline-size. The default — most dialogs (short forms, detailed confirmations).
theme.dialog.story.sample.size-body-lg: 720 px max-inline-size. For rich content — settings, multi-fields, previews.
