theme.dialog.story.sample.long-body-p2: The service is provided "as is" without express or implied warranty. We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time; modifications take effect upon publication on this page.
theme.dialog.story.sample.long-body-p3: You are solely responsible for the use you make of the service and the content you publish on it. You agree not to infringe the rights of third parties, particularly with respect to intellectual property, privacy or data protection.
theme.dialog.story.sample.long-body-p4: Any data you entrust to us is processed in accordance with our privacy policy, accessible from the footer. You can at any time exercise your rights of access, rectification, portability and deletion.
theme.dialog.story.sample.long-body-p5: This contract is governed by French law. In case of dispute, the parties will endeavor to find an amicable solution; failing that, the competent courts will be seized.
theme.dialog.story.sample.events-body: Open and close this dialog several times. The log captures every dialog:open / dialog:close.
theme.dropdown-menu.story.title: DropdownMenu — Popover + N Item + role=menu
theme.dropdown-menu.story.subtitle: Composes Popover (positioning + dismiss) and the `dropdown-menu` controller (role=menu + role=menuitem stamping + roving tabindex + Arrow Up/Down + Home/End + Enter/Space activate + close-on-item-click). The WAI-ARIA APG "Menu Button" pattern fully covered. Items rendered via Item.html.twig with `as: 'div'` — the controller stamps role/tabindex automatically.
theme.dropdown-menu.story.explainer.defa: Ghost Button trigger opens a menu of 3 text items. Click on an item closes the menu and runs the consumer's data-action (the menu controller's close-on-click runs AFTER the consumer's handler — bubble phase).
theme.dropdown-menu.story.explainer.with: Items with iconLeading + label — the most common pattern for contextual menus (actions / favorites / settings).
Items with trailing kbdShortcut — command palette / settings shortcut pattern. The shortcut is displayed; the global binding is consumer-side.
theme.dropdown-menu.story.explainer.grou: Items grouped via `<div class='theme-dropdown-menu__group-label'>` + separator `<hr class='theme-dropdown-menu__separator'>`. Keyboard nav naturally skips non-items.
theme.dropdown-menu.story.explainer.disa: Items marked `aria-disabled='true'`: visual dim + non-clickable (pass-through) + skipped from Arrow nav.
theme.dropdown-menu.story.explainer.plac: The menu inherits Popover's 4 logical placements (top/bottom/start/end) with auto-flip via Floating UI.
theme.alert-dialog.story.title: AlertDialog — destructive confirm with explicit commit policy
theme.alert-dialog.story.subtitle: Wrapper around Dialog that bakes the 3 destructive rules: Esc disabled, backdrop click disabled, no close-X. The user MUST click a button (Cancel or Confirm). Tone-aware leading icon (alert-triangle danger / alert-circle warning / info info). Reuses the dialog controller — no new JS.
theme.alert-dialog.story.explainer.dange: Default pattern: destructive Button trigger → AlertDialog opens with alert-triangle danger-soft icon + consequence-naming title + factual body + footer cluster [Cancel secondary][Delete permanently destructive]. Esc / backdrop click do NOT dismiss — the user MUST commit or cancel explicitly.
theme.alert-dialog.story.explainer.warni: Warning tone for non-destructive but consequential actions (archive, cancel order). Alert-circle warning-soft icon. Confirm typically primary (not destructive).
theme.alert-dialog.story.explainer.info: Info tone for neutral confirmations with informational impact. Info icon, info-soft. Confirm primary. Esc stays disabled for consistency — this is an explicit decision the user takes.
theme.alert-dialog.story.explainer.multi: Per 12-microcopy-tone §Confirmations: the body NAMES every consequence with a precise count. The user reads before clicking. This action will delete 47 files, 12 comments and 3 shares.
theme.alert-dialog.story.explainer.event: AlertDialog reuses the dialog controller, so events are dialog:open / dialog:close (bubbles + composed). Consumer wires a listener at document level for analytics OR on the wrap.
theme.alert-dialog.story.sample.danger-b: This action is irreversible. The project and its 142 associated tasks will be permanently lost.
theme.alert-dialog.story.sample.warning-: The project will be hidden from default lists. You can restore it anytime from archives.
theme.alert-dialog.story.sample.info-bod: The project will move to the closed-projects section. No data will be lost.
theme.alert-dialog.story.sample.multi-bo: This action will delete 47 files, 12 comments and 3 associated shares. Nothing can be restored.
theme.alert-dialog.story.sample.events-b: Open or close via the buttons: dialog:open and dialog:close accumulate in the log below.
theme.alert-dialog.story.sample.events-l: (no events yet — click a trigger above)
theme.drawer.story.title: Drawer — modal side panel sliding from a viewport edge
theme.drawer.story.subtitle: Composes Dialog's modal mechanics (native showModal, focus trap, Esc dismiss) + CSS-only slide-in/out via transform translate per edge. 4 logical edges (start / end / top / bottom) — start/end flip in RTL via --theme-drawer-flip custom property. No new motion tokens: reuse duration-slow emphasized for entry + duration-base accelerate for exit (per OQ-25).
theme.drawer.story.explainer.default: Default pattern: primary Button trigger → drawer opens from the inline-end (right LTR / left RTL). Native showModal() + ::backdrop. Esc + backdrop click close. Focus snapshot+restore via the controller (inherited from Dialog).
theme.drawer.story.explainer.edges: 4 logical edges. start/end use inset-inline-* (auto-flip per dir) + transform translate3d with --theme-drawer-flip (1 LTR / -1 RTL) to invert the entry direction. Top/bottom use negative/positive translateY (dir-agnostic).
theme.drawer.story.explainer.with-form: Native <dialog> focus trap cycles Tab between inputs + buttons inside. No JS trap needed. Aligns with Dialog doctrine OQ-22.
theme.drawer.story.explainer.long-conten: Drawer header + footer don't scroll (flex 0 0 auto). The body is flex 1 1 auto + overflow auto — it scrolls internally. Same pattern as shadcn / Radix.
theme.drawer.story.explainer.destructive: For critical workflow drawers (multi-step form not yet submitted), bake closeOnEscape=false + closeOnBackdropClick=false. The user MUST confirm cancel via the explicit close-X (or a Cancel button in the footer).
theme.drawer.story.explainer.events: The controller dispatches drawer:open / drawer:close (bubbles + composed). detail.edge contains the active edge. Consumer wires a listener at document level for analytics OR on the wrap.
theme.drawer.story.sample.default-body: Drawer appears from the end edge (right in LTR). showModal() positions it in the top-layer; the page behind is non-scrollable (native focus trap).
theme.drawer.story.sample.edges-body-sta: Appears from inline-start: left in LTR, right in RTL (flip via --theme-drawer-flip custom property).
theme.drawer.story.sample.edges-body-end: Appears from inline-end: right in LTR, left in RTL.
theme.drawer.story.sample.edges-body-top: Appears from the top. Direction-agnostic in RTL.
theme.drawer.story.sample.edges-body-bot: Appears from the bottom. Direction-agnostic in RTL.
theme.drawer.story.sample.long-body-p1: The drawer body is flex 1 1 auto + overflow auto — it scrolls internally when content overflows. Header stays pinned to the top, footer to the bottom.
theme.drawer.story.sample.long-body-p2: This pattern keeps an action cluster (Save / Cancel) always visible even when reading a long policy.
theme.drawer.story.sample.long-body-p3: The page behind the drawer stays non-scrollable thanks to showModal()'s native focus trap. No additional JS required.
theme.drawer.story.sample.long-body-p4: On mobile (Sheet variant in S2.2), the drawer becomes an automatic bottom-sheet under 768 px (OQ-26 doctrine).
theme.drawer.story.sample.long-body-p5: Honors logical CSS doctrine: no physical exception needed (slide direction is resolved CSS-time via inset-inline-* + --theme-drawer-flip).
theme.drawer.story.sample.destructive-bo: Esc and backdrop click are disabled on this drawer (closeOnEscape=false + closeOnBackdropClick=false). The user MUST confirm cancel via the explicit close-X or the Cancel button. Prevents accidental data loss in multi-step forms.
theme.drawer.story.sample.events-body: Open and close via the buttons: drawer:open (detail.edge) and drawer:close accumulate in the log below.
theme.drawer.story.sample.events-log-emp: (no events yet — click a trigger above)
theme.sheet.story.title: Sheet — responsive variant of Drawer (bottom-sheet mobile / side-drawer desktop)
theme.sheet.story.subtitle: Per OQ-26 doctrine (sealed S2.2 opening): responsive pivot at 768 px (Tailwind md). Desktop ≥ 768 px → behaves like Drawer with configured edge; mobile < 768 px → forces bottom-sheet semantics regardless of edge. Switch is CSS-only via @media query — controller agnostic of viewport (events sheet:open / sheet:close emit the configured edge, not the rendered edge).
theme.sheet.story.explainer.default: Default pattern: primary trigger → sheet opens from inline-end on desktop, from bottom on mobile (< 768 px). The data-edge config stays 'end' in all cases, but the CSS @media (max-width: 767.98px) overrides pinning to force bottom-sheet.
theme.sheet.story.explainer.edges: On desktop (≥ 768 px): behaves like Drawer with chosen edge. On mobile (< 768 px): all edges converge to bottom-sheet (the drag handle appears). The edge detail is still reported in the sheet:open event.
theme.sheet.story.explainer.with-form: Action sheet pattern: on mobile, present a short list of actions as a bottom-sheet. Compose Sheet + Items (Ring 1) for the action grid. Native focus trap cycles Tab between items.
theme.sheet.story.explainer.long-content: On mobile, the sheet never exceeds 85dvh (leaves air above to distinguish from fullscreen). Body scrolls internally when content overflows — header + footer pinned.
theme.sheet.story.explainer.events: Sheet dispatches sheet:open (detail.edge = configured edge) and sheet:close. The responsive rendering is invisible to events — useful for analytics measuring intent (side-drawer demanded), not the visual rendered (bottom-sheet on mobile).
theme.sheet.story.sample.default-body: On desktop you see a side-drawer end; resize under 768 px to see the auto-switch to bottom-sheet (drag handle appears).
theme.sheet.story.sample.edges-body: On mobile (< 768 px) this edge converges to bottom-sheet; on desktop, it behaves like Drawer.
theme.sheet.story.sample.long-body-p1: On mobile, the sheet caps at 85dvh — leaves air above to visualize the backdrop (modal signal). Body scrolls internally without hiding header + footer.
theme.sheet.story.sample.long-body-p2: The bottom-sheet pattern is canonical iOS / Material — feels like pulling a sheet of paper from the bottom. On desktop, the pattern becomes side-drawer (tucked neatly to the side).
theme.sheet.story.sample.long-body-p3: The 768 px breakpoint (OQ-26) matches Tailwind md — no isolated magic number. If a project prefers 640 px (sm) or 1024 px (lg), a brand preset can override the @media query.
theme.sheet.story.sample.long-body-p4: The drag handle (the small rounded bar at the top of the bottom-sheet) visually signals the 'swipe to dismiss' affordance — but doesn't do drag-to-dismiss at primitive level (deferred Phase 4 on demand).
theme.sheet.story.sample.long-body-p5: Backdrop tap closes (unless closeOnBackdropClick=false). Native focus trap + Esc dismiss + focus return are identical to Dialog / Drawer (controller inheritance).
theme.sheet.story.sample.events-body: Open and close: sheet:open (with detail.edge) and sheet:close accumulate in the log. Resizing under 768 px does NOT change the events — the reported edge stays the configured edge.
theme.sheet.story.sample.events-log-empt: (no events yet — click a trigger above)
theme.context-menu.story.title: ContextMenu — DropdownMenu twin via right-click
theme.context-menu.story.subtitle: Composes Popover (positioning) + DropdownMenu (role=menu + roving + Arrow nav) + context-menu (contextmenu trigger + phantom anchor positioned at clientX/Y). The browser's native menu is suppressed inside the configured area — accepted trade-off for in-app menus à la Notion / Linear / Figma. WAI-ARIA APG « Menu Button » fully covered.
theme.context-menu.story.explainer.defau: Right-click anywhere in the dashed area to open the menu at the pointer location. The browser's native menu is suppressed here — back/forward/inspect aren't available inside (accept the trade-off).
theme.context-menu.story.explainer.icons: Classic desktop-app pattern : edit verbs with icons and shortcuts. The separator isolates « Find » from the cut/copy/paste cluster.
theme.context-menu.story.explainer.group: « Edit » cluster + « Organize » cluster + trailing destructive action. The « View » item is disabled — excluded from Arrow nav and doesn't close the menu when clicked.
theme.context-menu.story.explainer.paren: area='parent' : right-click works on the wrap's parent element. Typical idiom : the menu is markup-nested inside `<tr>` for layout, but the right-click fires anywhere on the row.
theme.context-menu.story.explainer.event: The controller emits `context-menu:open` (with detail.{x, y} — the click coords) BEFORE popover.open() runs — consumers can mutate items synchronously just before display. And `context-menu:close` after dismiss.
theme.hover-card.story.title: HoverCard — visual preview via hover (with delay)
theme.hover-card.story.subtitle: Composes Popover (positioning + animation + Esc/click-outside dismiss) and the `hover-card` controller (mouseenter/mouseleave with 400/200 ms delays — Tooltip doctrine, no new motion token per OQ-29). HoverCard is a *visual preview* — does NOT open on focus (≠ Tooltip which describes). Esc dismiss preserved for keyboard recovery.
theme.hover-card.story.explainer.default: Hovering the @mention triggers open after 400 ms (anti-flash). Mouseleave triggers close after 200 ms — gives the pointer time to transit to the content without losing the card.
theme.hover-card.story.explainer.bio: Bottom placement (under the mention) with a short bio. Floating UI flips if the viewport edge forces a flip.
theme.hover-card.story.explainer.actions: Classic « profile card » pattern : avatar + name + role + bio + Follow/Message footer. Users can navigate inside the content (buttons are keyboard-actionable once the card is opened by hover).
theme.hover-card.story.explainer.long: Right placement (inline-end) with multi-paragraph. Max-inline-size 320 px ensures clean wrapping.
theme.hover-card.story.explainer.snappy: Custom 100 ms open + 50 ms close delays — for contexts where preview availability matters more than anti-flash. Override via data-hover-card-{open,close}-delay-value.
theme.hover-card.story.sample.default-su: with support from the design team.
theme.hover-card.story.sample.default-bi: Product designer at Acme since 2022. Passionate about design systems and accessibility.
theme.hover-card.story.sample.bio-body: Leads the frontend team since 2021. Accessibility + i18n specialist. In-house mentor on Symfony / Stimulus patterns.
theme.hover-card.story.sample.long-suffi: summarizes recent advances in design system practice.
theme.hover-card.story.sample.long-title: Galaxy design tokens : one year in
theme.hover-card.story.sample.long-body-: A retrospective on rolling out a token system in a team of 30 designers and 80 developers. The numbers, the pitfalls, the patterns that emerged.
Also covers the Tailwind v3 → v4 migration, oklch adoption, and the CI guards that saved coherence.
theme.hover-card.story.sample.snappy-suf: — the card opens almost without delay.
theme.hover-card.story.sample.snappy-bod: openDelay 100 ms / closeDelay 50 ms — for power-user contexts where anti-flash is sacrificed for responsiveness.
theme.select.story.title: Select — single-select listbox via Popover
theme.select.story.subtitle: Trigger button styled like an Input + listbox via Popover. WAI-ARIA APG « Listbox (Collapsible) » — focus stays on trigger, aria-activedescendant points to the active option. Mandatory pattern for Combobox (S2.3b). Per OQ-28 : vanilla, no Tom Select adapter — deferred to Phase 4 if async/virtual/multi-select demand emerges at scale.
theme.select.story.explainer.default: Click or ArrowDown/Up/Enter/Space on the trigger opens the listbox. Options are rendered server-side via the `options: [{value, label}]` param. Hidden input keeps the value for form-data submit.
theme.select.story.explainer.ssr: value="en" passes to the Twig template — the trigger label shows « English », the option has aria-selected="true" + check icon trailing. On popover:open, activedescendant points to the pre-selected option.
theme.select.story.explainer.disabled: The « Archived » option is disabled — excluded from Arrow nav + doesn't select on click. Consistent with NativeSelect / RadioGroup doctrine.
theme.select.story.explainer.sizes: 3 sizes : sm 32 px, md 40 px (default — `--density-input-height`), lg 48 px. Touch override `min-block-size: 44px` under coarse pointer (consistent Input doctrine).
theme.select.story.explainer.field: Field-style composition : Label with * required + Select with aria-labelledby + aria-describedby for help text. Field forwarding pattern (S1.2 doctrine).
theme.select.story.sample.field-help: This language will be used for the interface and email notifications.
theme.combobox.story.title: Combobox — filterable listbox with an Input trigger
theme.combobox.story.subtitle: ComboboxController extends SelectController (per OQ-32) — Input + Popover + case-insensitive substring filter + results announcement via #theme-announcer. Per OQ-28 vanilla (no Tom Select). Per OQ-31 : shared announcer. WAI-ARIA APG « Combobox with listbox popup ».
theme.combobox.story.explainer.default: Focusing the input opens the listbox. Type to filter on case-insensitive substring. ArrowDown/Up navigates (clamp), Enter selects, Esc clears then closes.
theme.combobox.story.explainer.ssr: value=en passes to Twig — the input shows « English », the option has aria-selected. On popover:open, activedescendant points to the pre-selected option.
theme.combobox.story.explainer.filter: Type « fr » to see only Français. The results count is announced via #theme-announcer (sr-only aria-live polite — shared per OQ-31). Esc with non-empty query : clear ; Esc with empty query : close.
theme.combobox.story.explainer.sizes: 3 sizes : sm 32 px, md 40 px (default — density-input-height), lg 48 px. Touch override min-block-size 44 px under coarse pointer.
theme.combobox.story.explainer.field: Field-style composition : Label with * required + Combobox with aria-labelledby + aria-describedby for help text. Pattern S1.2.
theme.combobox.story.empty.head: No result for «
theme.combobox.story.sample.field-help: This language will be used for the interface and email notifications.
theme.menubar.story.title: Menubar — app-style menu bar
theme.menubar.story.subtitle: Pure orchestrator (~70 effective lines) coordinating N standalone DropdownMenu compounds. Per OQ-30 (sealed S2.3a) : per-submenu DropdownMenus + thin orchestrator. WAI-ARIA APG « Menubar (menu of menus) » — ArrowLeft/Right cyclic, Home/End jump, ArrowDown/Enter/Space opens submenu, Esc cascade close. Cascading-open when a submenu is open.
theme.menubar.story.section.default: Default (4 menus File / Edit / View / Help)
theme.menubar.story.explainer.default: Tab focuses the first trigger. ArrowRight/Left navigates cyclically. ArrowDown opens the active trigger's submenu (focus auto-moves to the first item via DropdownMenu's _onPopoverOpen).
theme.menubar.story.explainer.shortcuts: Items with kbdShortcut display their shortcut trailing. Consistent with command-palette pattern.
theme.menubar.story.explainer.cascade: Open File via ArrowDown, then ArrowRight : Edit opens cleanly (cascading-open). Matches macOS / Apple HIG menubar idiom.
theme.menubar.story.explainer.groups: Grouped items with overline labels + separators. Consistent with DropdownMenu's group pattern.
theme.menubar.story.explainer.disabled: The Edit trigger is disabled — excluded from roving (ArrowRight from File jumps directly to View).
theme.navigation-menu.story.title: NavigationMenu — web navigation bar
theme.navigation-menu.story.subtitle: Extends MenubarController verbatim (per OQ-30 sealed S2.3a). Difference : wrapped in <nav aria-label> landmark + submenu items as <a href> (per OQ-33 sealed S2.3b) + aria-current=page on active item. Mega-menu deferred to Ring 3 pattern:Nav-MegaMenu.
theme.navigation-menu.story.section.head: Full header composition (Brand + Nav + Actions)
theme.navigation-menu.story.explainer.de: Web SaaS idiom : 3 dropdown menus ; each submenu item is a real <a href>. Middle-click opens in new tab (per OQ-33).
theme.navigation-menu.story.explainer.cu: The « For startups » item carries aria-current=page + data-selected=true — bg primary-soft + foreground primary-soft-foreground.
theme.navigation-menu.story.explainer.ri: Enriched items with iconLeading + description (caption below label). Advanced nav pattern à la Stripe / Vercel.
theme.navigation-menu.story.explainer.ca: Open Solutions via ArrowDown, then ArrowRight : Pricing opens cleanly. Inherited from MenubarController (per OQ-30).
theme.navigation-menu.story.explainer.he: NavigationMenu centered in a full Header : Brand link on the left, Actions (signin/signup) on the right. Layout is consumer-managed.
theme.navigation-menu.story.desc.rich-st: The perfect plan to get started quickly.
theme.table.story.title: Table — structural HTML primitive
theme.table.story.subtitle: Pure CSS HTML <table>. Foundation for DataTable (S2.4b). Sortable th with aria-sort + chevrons (CSS-only mask-image). Density-driven cell padding. RTL via browser-native column reversal + logical text-align. Numeric cells with tabular-nums + text-align: end.
theme.table.story.explainer.default: Standard HTML structure: sr-only caption, thead, tbody. Hover row state (--color-hover-overlay). Last row without bottom border for clean edge.
theme.table.story.explainer.sortable: th[data-sortable=true] wraps the label in a <button> + aria-sort=ascending|descending|none. Chevron-up (ascending) / chevron-down (descending) / chevrons-up-down (none) via inline SVG mask-image. No JS — the consumer toggles aria-sort server-side or via DataTable.
theme.table.story.explainer.numeric: Numeric columns: tabular-nums + text-align: end. RTL: stays visually aligned with the column's logical-end edge (the column reverses in RTL automatically, but digits stay right-aligned).
theme.table.story.explainer.selected: data-selected="true" + aria-selected="true" on <tr>. Background --color-selected-bg + foreground --color-selected-foreground (primary-soft palette). DataTable will compose this visual via bulk-select Checkbox header.
theme.table.story.explainer.striped: data-variant="striped" — alternating row bg via :nth-child(2n). Combined with hover overlay and selected state, specificity order: selected > hover > stripe.
theme.table.story.explainer.sizes: sm = compact padding (spacing-1/3) + xs font-size; md = density-driven (default); lg = ample padding (spacing-4/5). Sizing complements data-density=cozy|compact on the parent container.
theme.table.story.column.name: Name
theme.pagination.story.title: Pagination — paged navigation
theme.pagination.story.subtitle: 4 nav buttons (first/prev/next/last) + inline page-size selector (per OQ-35 sealed S2.4 opening) + aria-live range « {from}-{to} of {total} ». Naturally composes into the DataTable footer. Pattern Stripe / Linear / Notion.
theme.pagination.story.explainer.default: Initial state: first / prev disabled (page 1) ; next / last enabled. The range « 1-25 of 142 items » uses Intl.PluralRules (singular / plural).
theme.pagination.story.explainer.mid: Mid-pagination (page 3 / 6) : all buttons enabled. Click to navigate ; the range updates via aria-live=polite.
theme.pagination.story.explainer.page-si: Change page size via the inline NativeSelect — the page-size change resets the current page to 1 (canonical UX). The pagination:change event is dispatched.
theme.pagination.story.explainer.empty: totalItems = 0 → range « 0-0 of 0 items » + all nav buttons disabled. Consumers often place the Empty primitive above the pagination in this case.
theme.pagination.story.explainer.single: Single page (totalItems < pageSize) → all nav buttons disabled. Consumers can choose to hide Pagination entirely via a server-side check.
theme.pagination.story.explainer.events: Open the console or watch the log below : each navigation dispatches a CustomEvent « pagination:change » with detail.{page, pageSize, from, to, total, totalPages}.
theme.pagination.story.aria.label: Pagination
theme.scroll-area.story.title: ScrollArea — styled scrollable container
theme.scroll-area.story.subtitle: Pure CSS, 0 controller. Custom scrollbar via ::-webkit-scrollbar (Chromium/WebKit) + scrollbar-width / scrollbar-color (Firefox). Preserves native momentum scroll on iOS/Android. Direction-aware natively (browser flip in RTL).
theme.scroll-area.story.explainer.vertic: maxBlockSize=240px caps the height. Content overflows → vertical scrollbar appears. orientation=vertical explicitly clamps overflow-inline to hidden.
theme.scroll-area.story.explainer.horizo: Content is 1600 px inline (8 columns × 200 px). orientation=horizontal → horizontal scrollbar + overflow-block hidden. In RTL, scroll starts from inline-end (= visually left).
theme.scroll-area.story.explainer.both: maxBlockSize + maxInlineSize cap both axes. Content overflows in both → two scrollbars + a scrollbar-corner (transparent).
theme.scroll-area.story.explainer.sizes: sm 6 px (dense audit-log) ; md 10 px (default) ; lg 14 px (touch-friendly). Firefox's `scrollbar-width: thin|auto` is less granular than `::-webkit-scrollbar` but consistent.
theme.scroll-area.story.explainer.in-car: ScrollArea composed inside a Card : sticky-friendly header, scrollable body. Typical pattern for list panels (NotificationCenter, CommentsThread Ring 3).
theme.scroll-area.story.col.label: Column
theme.scroll-area.story.item.label: Item
theme.scroll-area.story.card.title: Recent notifications
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.1: The dashboard centralises key performance indicators and recent notifications. Advanced filters isolate a cohort or a period.
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.2: The page editor enables visual block composition, with real-time preview and version history. Changes are auto-saved.
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.3: Notification preferences offer granular matrices: per channel (email / push / in-app) and per category (commercial / system / security).
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.4: The danger zone groups irreversible actions: account suspension, permanent deletion, sign-out from all devices. Each requires explicit confirmation.
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.5: The audit log keeps the last 90 days of activity by default, exportable as CSV or JSON. Filters include action, resource, user, IP, and time range.
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.6: The help center provides a guided journey for new users, with onboarding checklist, short videos, and contextual FAQ.
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.7: OAuth integrations show requested permissions before connecting. Revocation is available anytime via the Connected integrations panel.
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.8: The Billing page shows historical invoices, the active payment method, and monthly usage. PDF exports are available per invoice.
theme.scroll-area.story.lorem.short: A short paragraph to illustrate column content.
theme.resizable.story.title: Resizable — 2-pane resizable container
theme.resizable.story.subtitle: CSS Grid 2-pane + handle drag (pointer + Arrow keys ±step% + Home/End). Per OQ-36 (sealed S2.4 opening): event-only via resizable:change ; consumer chooses persistence. WAI-ARIA APG « Window Splitter »: role=separator + aria-orientation + aria-valuenow/min/max. Grip CSS-only (3-dot vertical, mask-image).
theme.resizable.story.explainer.horizont: Drag the vertical handle with the mouse OR focus + ArrowLeft/Right to resize ±5%. Home → minSize, End → maxSize. Touch ≥44 px hit-area via transparent padding-inline.
theme.resizable.story.explainer.vertical: Same pivot but block-axis. ArrowUp/Down resize ; the handle is horizontal. Terminal-app pattern (output above, input below).
theme.resizable.story.explainer.clamp: minSize=20, maxSize=70. Drag or Arrow keys clamp within [20,70] %. At boundary, no more resizable:change dispatched (controller detects no-op).
theme.resizable.story.explainer.editor: Pattern IDE / Markdown editor / Code playground : editor 25-75 %, preview alongside. Vertical-split on mobile recommended (consumer-side adaptation via media query).
theme.resizable.story.explainer.events: Drag the handle (or Arrow keys focus-on-handle) — each drag-commit dispatches resizable:change with detail.{size, orientation}. Consumer wires localStorage / cookie / URL to persist.
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-left: Left pane. Drag the separator handle or focus the handle then Arrow keys / Home / End.
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-right: Right pane. Receives whatever inline-axis space remains (1fr in grid).
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-top: Top pane. Drag the horizontal handle to resize.
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-bottom: Bottom pane. Ideal for terminal output + input.
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-constrai: Min 20 %, max 70 %. At boundary, no further resizable:change events.
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-flexible: Receives the remainder. Its size is computed dynamically by CSS Grid (1fr).
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-editor: Edit zone. In real use, compose ScrollArea + CodeMirror / Monaco / TinyMCE.
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-preview: Preview zone. Renders the editor's live output (markdown, HTML, code preview, etc.).
theme.resizable.story.pane.body-demo: Watch the log below while you drag.
theme.data-table.bulk-destructive.body.o: This action is irreversible. {count} item will be permanently deleted.
This action is irreversible. {count} items will be permanently deleted.
theme.data-table.story.title: DataTable — workhorse data-grid orchestrator
theme.data-table.story.subtitle: Composes Table + Pagination + Checkbox indeterminate + DropdownMenu + AlertDialog + Empty. Vanilla per OQ-34 (no Tabulator). Single-col sort by default + opt-in multi-col (OQ-37). Text Input filter by default (OQ-38). AlertDialog for bulk-destructive (OQ-39 ; Sonner Toast-Undo deferred S2.5+).
theme.data-table.story.section.empty: Empty state (filtered no-results via Empty primitive)
theme.data-table.story.explainer.default: 5 columns (Name + Email + Role + Score + Status), Name/Email/Score sortable, multi-selection via per-row Checkbox + select-all header with indeterminate state (S1.3 doctrine). Bulk actions Export (ghost) + Delete (destructive). Pagination footer with inline page-size.
theme.data-table.story.explainer.column-: Score + Role are hidden by default (initial visibleColumns array). Click the 'Columns' trigger to open the DropdownMenu — toggle a checkbox to show/hide a column. The hidden attribute is applied to every matching <th> + <td>.
theme.data-table.story.explainer.filter: 3 columns with filterType=text — one input per column in a second tr inside <thead>. The 'Name' filter input is pre-filled with 'Marie' to demonstrate the filter-active state. The controller does NOT filter rows itself (data-source concern) — it emits data-table:filter-change with detail.{column, query, allFilters}.
theme.data-table.story.explainer.empty: When rows=[], a <tr><td colspan="6"> renders the Empty primitive size=sm with a search icon + microcopy 'No results. Clear filters.' + secondary CTA. Visually distinct from 'first-time no-data' (which invites creation, not filter clearing).
theme.data-table.story.explainer.bulk-de: 3 rows pre-selected → bulk-bar visible ('3 items selected') + 'Delete' destructive button. Click Delete → AlertDialog opens with the count named in the title + body ('Delete 3 items? This action is irreversible. 3 items will be permanently deleted.'). Esc + backdrop click are disabled (closeOnEscape=false + closeOnBackdropClick=false) — explicit-commit doctrine.
theme.data-table.story.explainer.events: Wired to all 5 dispatched events: sort-change, selection-change, column-visibility-change, bulk-action, filter-change. Click / select / open menu / type filter — events accumulate in the log with their payloads.
theme.data-table.story.explainer.control: Cross-controller compose Ring 2 amend per ADR-0048 — Twig param controllerExtras: ['sortable'] on the wrap renders data-controller="data-table sortable". DataTable controller + sortable controller co-mount on the same <div> wrap. Threshold REACHED at S4.2b CLOSE (10 instances parent-child cross-controller compose). Backward-compat: default controllerExtras = [] preserves data-controller="data-table" unchanged.
theme.data-table.story.column.name: Name
theme.data-table.story.empty.title: No results
theme.sonner.story.title: Sonner — ephemeral notifications queue
theme.sonner.story.subtitle: Toast engine with queue management, per-variant durations, pause-on-hover, and undo. Push via window.themeToast.show() or document.dispatchEvent('theme:toast:show').
theme.sonner.story.explainer.variants: Four pre-configured variants with default durations: success 4 s, info persistent, warning 6 s, danger persistent.
theme.sonner.story.explainer.undo: Soft-delete pattern: duration bumped to 10 s so the user has time to click Undo. The consumer callback receives the event and restores state.
theme.sonner.story.explainer.stack: Beyond 5 visible toasts, additional ones queue up. A '+N more' indicator shows the overflow ; click to expand.
theme.sonner.story.explainer.pause: Hover the viewport to freeze every visible toast's timer. Leave the viewport and each timer resumes with its remaining time.
theme.sonner.story.explainer.esc: Esc from anywhere dismisses every visible toast and clears the queue.
theme.sonner.story.explainer.events: Three events bubble + composed from the viewport: sonner:show, sonner:dismiss, sonner:undo. Attach a document-level listener for analytics.
theme.sonner.story.sample.warning-messag: Your invite link expires in 24 h.
theme.sonner.story.sample.danger-message: Try again in a moment, or contact support.
theme.sonner.story.sample.pause-hint: Tip: hover the viewport to freeze the timer. Leave and the countdown resumes.
theme.sonner.story.sample.esc-hint: Tip: press Esc to dismiss every visible toast.
theme.sonner.story.sample.events-log-emp: (nothing yet — push a toast to start)
theme.toast.story.title: Toast — simplified consumer API
theme.toast.story.subtitle: Public wrapper on top of Sonner. Two surfaces: window.themeToast.show() (preferred) or document.dispatchEvent('theme:toast:show') (no-coupling).
theme.toast.story.explainer.js-api: Call window.themeToast.show({ variant, message, … }) after boot(). Synchronous, fire-and-forget.
theme.toast.story.explainer.event-api: Dispatch a CustomEvent on document with detail = { variant, message, … }. Useful for consumers that cannot import the kit's exports.
theme.description-list.story.title: DescriptionList — semantic term/definition pairs
theme.description-list.story.subtitle: Composes native <dl><dt><dd> with two layouts: stacked (default) or side-by-side. Zero controller, pure CSS.
theme.description-list.story.section.sta: Term sits above its definition. Larger gap between pairs than between dt and dd. Best for mobile views or contexts where definitions are long.
theme.description-list.story.section.sid: CSS Grid 2 columns: term in inline-start column (max-content), definition in inline-end column (1fr). Baseline-aligned. Best for desktop views summarizing metadata.
theme.description-list.story.section.ric: The definition accepts raw HTML (Badge, link, icon, etc.). Twig | raw filter — sanitization is the consumer's responsibility.
theme.description-list.story.section.lon: For side-by-side: the max-content column lets terms grow without truncation. Wrap remains possible if the window narrows. No hidden overflow.
theme.description-list.story.section.car: Composed inside a Card (settings panel pattern)
Typical integration pattern: DescriptionList as the body of a Card with header. Pure composition of Ring 1 (Card) + Ring 2 (DescriptionList).
theme.description-list.story.card.title: Organization profile
theme.avatar-group.story.title: AvatarGroup — visual stack of Avatars + overflow indicator
theme.avatar-group.story.subtitle: Composes Avatar via consumer items. CSS-only overlap via flex + negative margin-inline-start. The '+N' indicator uses Intl.PluralRules for the accessible label (consumer pre-resolves). Zero controller.
theme.avatar-group.story.section.default: max=4 (default). The 4 avatars stack with a 2 px border separating each member. The group label is consumer-resolved via Intl.ListFormat ("Marie Dupont, Alex Bernard, and Théo Laurent").
theme.avatar-group.story.section.overflo: If avatars.length > max, render max-1 avatars + 1 '+N' indicator (Stripe / Slack pattern). The indicator reuses the avatar circle (size + radius) with bg-sunken + text-tertiary so it reads as 'rest'. aria-label resolved via Intl.PluralRules — '1 more member' (CLDR EN `one`) vs 'N more members' (CLDR EN `other`).
theme.avatar-group.story.section.sizes-e: All Avatar sizes work. Overlap is 25 % of the avatar size (24 px → 6 px overlap, 80 px → 20 px). The indicator inherits the size (border + circle).
theme.avatar-group.story.section.status-: Each avatar can carry its status dot independently. Dots stay visible at the inline-end / block-end of each avatar — the overlap (≤ 25 %) leaves the corner visible.
theme.avatar-group.story.section.single-: 1 avatar, max=4. No overflow, no indicator. The wrapper renders visually as an isolated Avatar (the inline-flex + 2 px border remain — for visual consistency if the count grows dynamically).
theme.status.story.title: Status — live indicator with dot + opt-in pulse
theme.status.story.subtitle: Composes the Badge soft envelope + a leading variant-colored dot. variant=danger → role=alert (assertive) ; other variants → implicit <output> role=status (polite). Pulse is opt-in CSS-only at 0.67 Hz (well under the 3 Hz epilepsy floor).
theme.status.story.section.variants-expl: Composes the Badge soft vocabulary (color-{variant}-soft + soft-foreground). The dot reuses the variant's solid color at full intensity for max contrast over the soft pill. The label is read inline by the screen reader.
theme.status.story.section.pulse-explain: data-pulse="true" enables a CSS-only animation on the dot — a ring scaling 1→2.5 with opacity 0.6→0 over 1500 ms infinite ease-out. ~0.67 Hz cadence, well under the 3 Hz epilepsy floor ([13-a11y §15]). For 'live' contexts (uptime monitoring, active queue, real-time presence).
theme.status.story.section.sizes-explain: sm = padding-block 1 px + dot 6 px + font 2xs ; md (default) = padding-block --spacing-0_5 + dot 8 px + font caption. Pulse works in both sizes.
theme.status.story.section.inline-explai: Status works as an inline composition with body text. vertical-align: middle aligns the pill on the parent text's baseline. Best for 'system status' pages summarizing service health.
theme.status.story.section.reduced-expla: Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, the pulse is disabled (animation: none + opacity 0 on the ring). The dot stays solid — color alone communicates the state.
theme.status.story.section.reduced-os-ti: macOS: Settings → Accessibility → Display → Reduce motion. Windows: Settings → Accessibility → Visual effects → Animation effects. Linux GNOME: gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface enable-animations false.
theme.tag.story.title: Tag — labelled chip with optional icon + opt-in dismiss
theme.tag.story.subtitle: Composes the Badge envelope (6 variants × {solid, soft}). 3 sizes (sm / md / lg). Dismissable opt-in via dismissable=true (mirrors alert-dismiss S1.4b verbatim — transitionend opacity filter for cleanup).
theme.tag.story.section.sizes-explainer: 3 sizes — sm for dense contexts (table cells, breadcrumbs), md (default) for general use, lg for prominent filter pills.
theme.tag.story.section.dismiss-explaine: The × button wires the tag-dismiss controller. Click → data-state="dismissing" → opacity 1→0 over 120 ms → DOM remove + dispatch tag:dismissed (detail.label) + announcer push "Tag X removed". Mirrors the alert-dismiss S1.4b pattern verbatim.
theme.tag.story.section.filter-explainer: Tag-filter pattern: active selection materialized as a cluster of dismissable pills. Each dismiss fires tag:dismissed for the consumer to update state.
theme.number-input.story.title: NumberInput — touch-friendly +/- stepper around <input type=number>
theme.number-input.story.subtitle: Composes the Input visual + 2 stepper buttons. Keyboard stays 100 % native (Arrow Up/Down on the input). The +/- buttons invoke input.stepUp() / stepDown() — the browser respects min / max / step. Touch-friendly via @media (pointer: coarse) → buttons ≥ 44 × 44 px.
theme.number-input.story.section.default: Without min/max, +/- increment / decrement indefinitely. The placeholder shows when the input is empty. Type-to-edit remains available.
theme.number-input.story.section.min-max: With min / max — buttons disabled at the edges

When value === min, the − button becomes disabled (greyed + cursor: not-allowed). When value === max, the + button becomes disabled. The opposite button stays active. The user sees the bounds immediately.
theme.number-input.story.section.step-ex: step="5": each +/- click moves by 5 units. step="0.01": decimals (typical for monetary prices). The value stays aligned to the step.
theme.number-input.story.section.sizes-e: sm = 32 px buttons (compact tables). md (default) = --density-input-height (40 px default, 36 cozy, 28 compact). lg = 48 px (e-commerce / finance forms).
theme.number-input.story.section.field-e: Form pattern: Label + NumberInput + help. The focus-within on the wrap emits the ring on the whole group. aria-required + aria-describedby for a11y.
theme.number-input.story.field.label: Order quantity
theme.calendar.story.title: Calendar — month-grid date display
theme.calendar.story.subtitle: Foundation compound for DatePicker (S2.7 workhorse — coming). Renders a full month as a 7 × N grid (5 or 6 weeks). WAI-ARIA APG "Date Picker Dialog" / "Grid" pattern — role=grid + role=row + role=gridcell + roving tabindex + aria-activedescendant. Keyboard nav: ArrowLeft/Right ±1 day (chronological), ArrowUp/Down ±7 days, PageUp/Down ±1 month, Shift+PageUp/Down ±1 year, Home/End start/end of week, Enter/Space selects. Today highlight via aria-current=date. Cells out of min/max OR disabled-weekday OR other-month are aria-disabled. Locale-aware via Intl.DateTimeFormat (no Day.js dependency — deferred until relative-time UX surfaces in Ring 3+). Configurable week-start (0=Sunday, 1=Monday default for FR).
theme.calendar.story.section.default-exp: Minimal display — no pre-selected date. Today gets aria-current=date but is NOT selected — click or Enter on the focused cell selects. The focused cell has tabindex=0; others tabindex=-1 (roving).
theme.calendar.story.section.with-value-: value=ISO 8601 → that cell becomes aria-selected=true + data-state=selected (full primary background + primary-foreground text + semibold weight).
theme.calendar.story.section.min-max-exp: min + max bound the selectable days. Out-of-range cells: aria-disabled=true + greyed visual. Click no-op; ArrowLeft/Right does NOT skip these cells (per WAI-ARIA APG — user can navigate over but not select).
theme.calendar.story.section.disabled-we: disabledWeekdays="0,6" (Sunday + Saturday) → every sat/sun cell aria-disabled. Use case: business-days-only booking.
theme.calendar.story.section.locale-expl: Month label ("mai 2026" / "May 2026") and weekday headers (L M M J V S D / S M T W T F S) are localized via Intl.DateTimeFormat. No external lib dependency. Day.js deferred until relative-time UX (chatbot, activity feed) lands in Ring 3+.
theme.calendar.story.section.events-expl: calendar:select detail.{iso, date} on click or Enter on focused cell; calendar:display-month-change detail.{month, label} on prev/next nav. All bubbles + composed → consumer can listen on a parent.
theme.input-otp.story.title: InputOTP — one-time-code input (4-8 digits)
theme.input-otp.story.subtitle: N-cell atomic input cluster (default N=6, range 4-8) for OTP / verification codes / 2FA. Auto-tab next on input, backspace-on-empty moves focus prev + clears, paste auto-distribute (OQ-41 sealed S2.7 — Stripe / Linear / Vercel pattern). inputmode="numeric" triggers the numeric keyboard on iOS / Android; autocomplete="one-time-code" on the first input enables iOS SMS-OTP auto-fill (browser distributes the received code). Group ARIA semantics via fieldset + legend; each cell has an Intl-interpolated aria-label ("Digit 3 of 6"). Emits input-otp:change (every mutation) + input-otp:complete (when all cells filled).
theme.input-otp.story.section.default-ex: Most common pattern: 6 digits for email / SMS OTP. Type 1 digit → focus moves to next. Backspace on empty cell → focus jumps back + clears the previous.
theme.input-otp.story.section.length-4-e: Valid range: 4-8 digits. 4 = legacy card PIN. 6 = standard 2FA. 8 = backup code.
theme.input-otp.story.section.length-8-e: For backup codes / recovery seeds with stronger entropy.
theme.input-otp.story.section.paste-expl: Paste "123456" (or "12-34-56" / "12 34 56") into any cell → distributes one digit per cell. Non-numeric pastes (e.g. "abc") are ignored silently. Stripe / Linear pattern.
theme.input-otp.story.section.states-exp: disabled on the fieldset → all cells disabled (form won't submit). aria-invalid=true on the fieldset → danger border-color on every cell.
theme.input-otp.story.section.field-expl: Wrap InputOTP in Field for a visible label + help text. aria-describedby points at the help id. Keep the fieldset legend visible (group label) — don't hide it.
theme.input-otp.story.section.events-exp: input-otp:change detail.{code, complete} on every mutation; input-otp:complete detail.{code} only when all cells are filled (length === filled count + all numeric).
theme.input-otp.story.field.label: Verification code
theme.input-otp.story.field.help: You received this code by email.
theme.date-picker.story.title: DatePicker — Input-anchored date picker (pickers family workhorse)
theme.date-picker.story.subtitle: Composes Popover (S2.1) + Input trigger (S1.2) + Calendar (S2.7). Input read-only; click or ArrowDown or Enter opens the popover with the Calendar inside. Calendar.calendar:select → Input.value formatted via Intl.DateTimeFormat ("May 15, 2026" / "15 mai 2026" / "15/05/2026" per locale + dateStyle), hidden input ISO 8601, popover closes, date-picker:change dispatched. Lazy-loaded Floating UI per ADR-0011 → first popover open: 5-200 ms position-snap delay (acceptable on broadband, subsequent opens cached). Vanilla per OQ-40 — no Flatpickr adapter (deferred to future amend if power features land: multi-month, range, locale-specific calendars Hebrew/Hijri/Buddhist).
theme.date-picker.story.section.default-: Click the input or press ArrowDown/Enter → popover opens with the Calendar visible. Pick a day → input.value shows "15 mai 2026", hidden input carries "2026-05-15" for form submit.
theme.date-picker.story.section.formats-: dateStyle prop drives Intl.DateTimeFormat. 'long' = "May 15, 2026", 'medium' = "May 15, 2026" (compact), 'short' = "5/15/26", 'full' = "Friday, May 15, 2026". The hidden input always stays ISO 8601 yyyy-mm-dd.
theme.date-picker.story.section.locales-: locale prop drives everything: month name in the Calendar header ("May" / "mai"), weekday labels (S M T W T F S / L M M J V S D), Input format. Zero external dep — pure Intl.DateTimeFormat.
theme.date-picker.story.section.constrai: min + max bound the selectable days in the Calendar. Out-of-range cells aria-disabled + click no-op. If the initial value is out-of-range, the Calendar keeps it displayed (consumer's responsibility to validate before submit).
theme.date-picker.story.section.field-ex: DatePicker integrates into a theme-field via the standard Field pattern. aria-describedby links to the help id. aria-invalid on the wrap → danger border on the input.
theme.date-picker.story.section.events-e: date-picker:change detail.{iso, formatted, date: Date} on selection. Bubbles + composed → consumer can listen on a parent.
theme.date-picker.story.field.label: Meeting date
theme.date-picker.story.field.help: Pick a date in the next 30 days.
theme.tabs.story.title: Tabs — accessible WAI-ARIA APG tab interface
theme.tabs.story.subtitle: List of role=tab triggers + role=tabpanel panels. Default activation is automatic (Arrow keys → focus + select, Stripe / Vercel / Notion pattern); opt-in `activation=manual` for heavy-content tabs (Arrow keys → focus only, Enter / Space → activate). Roving tabindex + Home / End + cyclic Arrow nav.
theme.tabs.story.explainer.default: Click or Arrow Left / Right (cyclic) navigates + activates. Home / End jump to first / last. Tab key exits the tablist into the panel content. The « Billing » tab is disabled — excluded from keyboard nav.
theme.tabs.story.explainer.pill: Pill variant: the tablist gets a bg-sunken background, each trigger is centered, and the active one gains a bg-elevated fill + shadow-1 (subtle lift). No underline. ToggleGroup-like pattern, common for 2–4 filtering tabs.
theme.tabs.story.explainer.sizes: 3 sizes via data-size: sm (32 px / xs font), md (40 px / sm font, default), lg (48 px / base font). Under pointer:coarse (touch), min-block-size bumps to 44 px (a11y target floor §6).
theme.tabs.story.explainer.vertical: orientation=vertical: the list stacks block, the indicator becomes an inline-end border. Arrow Up / Down navigates (Arrow Left / Right is inactive). Idiom: settings page Sidebar-like (where Sidebar would be over-kill but 4–5 vertical tabs suffice).
theme.tabs.story.explainer.manual: activation=manual: Arrow keys move focus WITHOUT activating. Enter or Space activates the focused tab. Recommended when activation triggers a costly operation (data fetch, heavy render). Otherwise prefer the automatic default.
theme.tabs.story.explainer.events: Every activation dispatches a CustomEvent « tabs:change » with detail.{value, previousValue}. Bubbles + composed for consumer-side listening at any DOM tree depth.
theme.tabs.story.panel.profile: Configure your name, avatar, and bio. Changes save automatically after 2 seconds of inactivity.
theme.tabs.story.panel.pref: Choose your theme (light / dark / system), language, and density. Preferences are local to your account.
theme.tabs.story.panel.security: Password, 2FA, active sessions. Enable two-factor auth to protect your account from unauthorized access.
theme.tabs.story.panel.billing: Plan, payment method, invoices. (Disabled — your plan is managed by your organization administrator.)
theme.tabs.story.panel.size: The panel content is identical across sizes — only the trigger bar changes.
theme.tabs.story.panel.vertical-general: General preferences: organization name, default timezone, week start day.
theme.tabs.story.panel.vertical-notif: Email, push, in-app. Choose the preferred channel for each event category (mention, new member, broken build…).
theme.tabs.story.panel.vertical-lang: Display language and locale for dates, numbers, and currencies. Language also affects notification emails.
theme.tabs.story.panel.vertical-advanced: Technical settings: webhooks, API keys, audit log, GDPR export. Admin-only.
theme.tabs.story.panel.manual-overview: Aggregated dashboard. Numbers refresh every 5 minutes — no risk of re-fetching on every tab switch.
theme.tabs.story.panel.manual-reports: List of scheduled reports. Opening triggers an async request (~1 s) — that's why this tablist is in manual mode.
theme.tabs.story.panel.manual-export: Generate a CSV / Excel / PDF export. Activating the tab does NOT start the export — click the « Run export » button in the panel to begin.
theme.tabs.story.panel.events: Activate another tab above — the events log on the right records value + previousValue at each tabs:change.
theme.stepper.story.title: Stepper — linear progress indicator
theme.stepper.story.subtitle: Purely declarative Stepper: the consumer drives `currentStep` via the Stimulus value, the controller computes derived per-step state (completed / current / upcoming) + stamps aria-current="step" + dispatches stepper:step-change on transition. No setStep / next / prev API — consistent with the « no implicit side effects + consumer owns state » doctrine (alias OQ-44 Sidebar persist).
theme.stepper.story.explainer.default: Step 1 « Account » is completed (filled checkmark in primary); step 2 « Profile » is current (primary-bordered circle + bold label); steps 3-4 are upcoming (gray circle + tertiary label). The connector between completed steps is also primary.
theme.stepper.story.explainer.sizes: 3 sizes via data-size: sm (28 px indicator + xs font), md (36 px + sm, default), lg (44 px + base). Under pointer:coarse, the minimum bumps to 44 px (a11y target floor §6) except sm, which stays compact at 36 px.
theme.stepper.story.explainer.vertical: orientation=vertical: steps stack in a column, vertical connector between them (inline-start of the indicators), labels and descriptions aligned text-start. Idiom: multi-step checkout or KYC document flow.
theme.stepper.story.explainer.disabled: The « Payment » step is flagged disabled (opacity 0.5, excluded from the flow). The controller preserves the SSR-stamped data-state="disabled" — it doesn't override it with completed / current / upcoming.
theme.stepper.story.explainer.interactiv: Previous / Next buttons mutate ctrl.currentStepValue. The counter shows current / total. When current > total, the stepper:step-change event carries `completed: true` (visible in the log below).
theme.stepper.story.explainer.events: Every currentStep transition dispatches a CustomEvent stepper:step-change with detail.{currentStep, previousStep, totalSteps, completed: boolean}. The `completed` flag is true when currentStep > totalSteps (the consumer reached the end).
theme.stepper.story.desc.kyc: ID card or passport, both sides
theme.stepper.story.desc.review: Our team checks the documents within 24 business hours
theme.sidebar.story.title: Sidebar — vertical application navigation
theme.sidebar.story.subtitle: `<aside>` landmark with sections and items. Collapsed mode (icon-only, 64 px) toggled via the header button — labels become sr-only in collapsed mode, accessible names preserved. Per OQ-44 (sealed S2.8 opening): event-only persistence (consumer chooses localStorage / cookie / URL). Per OQ-26 (Sheet): mobile mode < 768 px = consumer composes Sheet for the drawer.
theme.sidebar.story.explainer.default: 2 sections (anonymous + « Administration »), 5 items, the active « Dashboard » carries aria-current="page" (consumer-stamped via SSR). Click the header button (panel-left-close icon) to switch to collapsed mode.
theme.sidebar.story.explainer.collapsed: Collapsed mode via data-sidebar-collapsed-value="true": width drops to 64 px, labels become sr-only (accessible name preserved for SR + hover / focus reveal via Tooltip if the consumer composes one). The toggle icon flips (panel-left-open).
theme.sidebar.story.explainer.footer: Footer slot via Twig param `footerContent` (raw HTML — consumer-owned sanitization per [01-overview §I-3]). Idiom: user menu trigger, quick sign-out, support link. In collapsed mode, the footer stays visible (shrinks visually with the wrap).
theme.sidebar.story.explainer.events: Click the toggle to watch sidebar:collapse-change detail.{collapsed} accumulate in the log. Bubbles + composed for consumer-side listening anywhere in the DOM tree. The consumer wires persistence (localStorage / cookie / URL — consistent with the OQ-36 / OQ-44 event-only doctrine).
theme.sidebar.story.shell.placeholder-bo: Sidebar at inline-start, main on the right (LTR) — classic app shell pattern. Toggle collapsed mode via the header button on the sidebar.
theme.sidebar.story.shell.collapsed-body: Sidebar at 64 px, icon-only. Hover an item to read its aria-label (consumer can compose Tooltip for the visual reveal — pattern documented in spec §).
theme.sidebar.story.shell.footer-body: The footer slot (bottom of the sidebar) accepts raw HTML — idiom: user menu trigger, support link, quick sign-out.
theme.carousel.story.title: Carousel — slide carousel with opt-in autoplay + touch swipe + keyboard nav
theme.carousel.story.subtitle: Per OQ-43 (sealed S2.8 opening): autoplay OFF by default, opt-in via data-carousel-autoplay-interval-value=<ms>; pause-on-pointerenter + pause-on-focuswithin; skip autoplay under prefers-reduced-motion; loop direction = back-to-first after last (cyclic, OQ-mini-43a). Touch swipe ≥ 50 px triggers prev/next. Keyboard: Arrow Left/Right + Home/End. WAI-ARIA APG Carousel: role=region + aria-roledescription=carousel + per-slide role=group + aria-roledescription=slide + aria-label « N of M ».
theme.carousel.story.explainer.default: 4 testimonials without autoplay. Click prev / next or the dots to navigate. Cyclic by default: prev on the first wraps to the last. Tab focus on the active slide, Arrow Left / Right to move.
theme.carousel.story.explainer.autoplay: autoplayInterval=5000 → tick every 5 s. Pause-on-pointerenter (hover) + pause-on-focuswithin (Tab inside). Skip autoplay under prefers-reduced-motion (DevTools → Emulate CSS prefers-reduced-motion). Play / pause button visible (WCAG SC 2.2.2 Pause/Stop/Hide).
theme.carousel.story.explainer.bounded: loop=false: prev disabled on slide 0, next disabled on the last. No wrap. Idiom: checkout step preview (each slide = a future step, no cyclic).
theme.carousel.story.explainer.keyboard: Focus the wrap or a slide, then: Arrow Left = prev (RTL inverse), Arrow Right = next (RTL inverse), Home = slide 0, End = last. Touch swipe ≥ 50 px triggers prev/next. Mouse drag also via setPointerCapture.
theme.carousel.story.explainer.events: Every slide change dispatches a CustomEvent carousel:slide-change with detail.{currentSlide, previousSlide, totalSlides, direction: 'next'|'prev'|'jump'}. Bubbles + composed.
theme.carousel.story.quote.t1: The kit saved us weeks on the redesign — coherent tokens, RTL day-1, automated CI gates.
theme.carousel.story.quote.t2: A11y baseline ticked for every component. Zero regressions across NVDA / VoiceOver audits.
theme.carousel.story.quote.t3: Stimulus + Twig + pure CSS tokens. No React runtime forced — we keep our Symfony stack.
theme.carousel.story.quote.t4: Sidebar's collapsed mode brought back readability on 13-inch laptops.
theme.carousel.story.feature.f1-body: RTL without rewriting a line — flip <html dir="rtl"> and you're done.
theme.carousel.story.feature.f2-body: One CSS variable, one preset swap, and the whole app reskins.
theme.carousel.story.feature.f3-body: axe-core green on every story, contrast matrix asserted in CI.
theme.chart.empty.title: No data
theme.chart.empty.description: No data points to display for this period.
theme.chart.error.title: Couldn't load the chart
theme.chart.error.description: Check your connection and try again.
theme.chart.story.title: Chart — data visualizations
theme.chart.story.subtitle: Lazy-loaded ApexCharts adapter — line, bar, donut, area, sparkline. Kit tokens applied at runtime (KPI palette, foreColor, tooltip theme). Per ADR-0012.
theme.chart.story.explainer.kpi: Four KPI cards with embedded sparklines. Sparkline renders in a 60 px container ; primary value, label and trend % occupy the meta block. Ring 4 Dashboard pattern.
theme.chart.story.explainer.line: 'line' variant with 2 series — year-over-year comparison. Smooth stroke, palette --color-kpi-{1,2}.
theme.chart.story.explainer.bar: 'bar' variant with single series. dataLabels off for visual density ; consumer wires per-category aria-label via the sr-only table.
theme.chart.story.explainer.donut: 'donut' variant — labels = categories, series = scalar values. Legend at the bottom. Tabular-nums on the percentages.
theme.chart.story.explainer.area: 'area' variant with two series (organic vs paid). Smooth curve. Composes the same 6 --color-kpi palette.
theme.chart.story.explainer.events: Click a bar to fire chart:series-click. The chart:render-complete fires after the lazy-load + render() resolve.
theme.chart.story.events.label: Bar with events
theme.chart.story.line.label: Monthly sales 2025 vs 2026
theme.chart.story.bar.label: Sales by category
theme.chart.story.donut.label: Audience breakdown
theme.chart.story.area.label: Monthly traffic by source
theme.file-upload.labels.idle: Drag your files here or click to browse
theme.file-upload.story.title: FileUpload — drop zone + multi-file + preview + progress
theme.file-upload.story.subtitle: Lazy-loaded FilePond adapter — native <input type="file"> + progressive FilePond drop zone. Composes Sonner via window.themeToast.show for upload-complete / upload-failed. Per ADR-0013.
theme.file-upload.story.explainer.defaul: Drop zone accepts any type ; multi-file ; max 10 MB per file. Most common pattern.
theme.file-upload.story.explainer.image: accept="image/*" + imagePreview=true → FilePond renders thumbnails after add. Multi-file.
theme.file-upload.story.explainer.single: maxFiles=1 → only one file accepted. Selecting a new file replaces the previous.
theme.file-upload.story.explainer.strict: accept="application/pdf" + maxFileSize="2MB". Demonstrates client-side rejection (server must re-validate).
theme.file-upload.story.explainer.events: Add a file to see file-upload:add. If you pass a server, file-upload:upload-progress and file-upload:upload-complete fire. Sonner toast composed via window.themeToast.show.
theme.command.empty.title: No results
theme.command.story.title: Command — ⌘K palette
theme.command.story.subtitle: Custom vanilla — cross-controller compose dialog + combobox + command on the same wrap (mirror DatePicker S2.7). Global hotkey ⌘K / Ctrl+K. Consumer registry via window.themeCommand.register. Per OQ-47.
theme.command.story.section.default: Default — ⌘K palette with groups + Kbd footer
theme.command.story.explainer.default: 8 commands across 3 groups (Navigation / Create / Help). Footer Kbd shows Navigate / Execute / Dismiss. Type in the input to filter.
theme.command.story.placeholder.default: Search a command, a project, a page…
theme.command.story.empty.title: No results
theme.auth-shell.story.title: _auth-shell — layout shell for Auth patterns
theme.auth-shell.story.subtitle: Sub-template shared by Auth-Login, Auth-Register, Auth-ForgotPassword, Auth-ResetPassword (Ring 3 S3.1) and the Auth-* extras (S3.1b/c). Twig {% embed %} composition with brand / illustration / content / footer blocks. <main> landmark + <article aria-labelledby> Card. 2 variants: default (single-column centered, mobile-first) and split (illustration aside + panel ≥ 1024 px).
theme.auth-shell.story.explainer.default: Canonical Auth layout: flex centered, min-block-size: 100dvh, panel max-inline-size 26rem (~416 px). The brand header + footer link block are opt-in via Twig blocks. Safe-area insets honored for iOS notch + bottom-bar.
theme.auth-shell.story.explainer.with-br: Without the footer link, the shell stays balanced — the card's bottom margin handles visual centering. Typical case: ForgotPassword + ResetPassword where no bottom-page redirect link is offered.
theme.auth-shell.story.explainer.split: Mobile-first: single-column (illustration hidden). From 1024 px, the aside fills 50 % of the viewport in primary-soft background. Consumer fills the illustration block (SVG / image / lottie). aria-hidden=true since decorative.
theme.auth-shell.story.sample.title: Sign in
theme.auth-shell.story.sample.subtitle: Welcome back.
theme.auth.login.title: Sign in
theme.auth.login.subtitle: Welcome back.
theme.auth.login.error.credentials: Email or password incorrect. Forgot password?
theme.auth.login.error.email-format: The email must contain an @ and a domain. For instance alice@samurai.com.
theme.auth.login.error.rate-limited: Too many attempts. Try again in {minutes} minutes.
theme.auth.login.story.title: Auth-Login — login pattern
theme.auth.login.story.subtitle: Login pattern (Ring 3 S3.1) — composes _auth-shell + Field + Input + Button + Checkbox + Alert. Pure server-rendered form (OQ-55); the consumer wires the submit handler natively (Symfony / Rails / Express). 0 Stimulus controller in S3.1.
theme.auth.login.story.explainer.default: Initial state: empty email + password, placeholders shown, primary submit enabled. Default focus goes to the first Input (browser-managed). CSRF token rendered as hidden input (consumer Symfony provides csrfToken Twig param).
theme.auth.login.story.explainer.server-: Server-flash: globalError Alert variant=danger at the top of the form. role=alert → SR announces immediately. The emailValue is preserved on re-render (the user doesn't lose their email entry). Pattern: <explanation>. <proposed action>. — the inline forgot link stays semantically active.
theme.auth.login.story.explainer.error-i: Inline Field error: red border + emailError paragraph + aria-invalid + aria-describedby. SR reads the error on field focus. The password Field stays default. Pattern: max 1 error per field (cross-field errors → globalError Alert).
theme.auth.login.story.explainer.rate-li: Rate-limited: Alert variant=warning + submit Button with aria-disabled=true (preserves focus reach for SR). The minutes count is consumer-interpolated via t() params. Live countdown deferred — defer Ring 3+ if demand emerges.
theme.auth.login.story.explainer.loading: Loading submit: Button data-loading=true → spinner replaces the icon slot, label stays 'Sign in' (width-stable per Button.md). aria-busy=true → SR announces 'busy'. The form is non-submittable natively (browser HTML5).
theme.auth.login.story.explainer.prefill: Server-side pre-fill: email recovered from the previous session, remember-me checked. Typical pattern: after-logout flash 'See you soon' redirects to /login with email preserved to make re-login easier. The password is NEVER pre-filled (security).
theme.auth.register.title: Create your account
theme.auth.register.subtitle: A few details and we're off.
theme.auth.register.name: Name
theme.auth.register.password-help: 8 characters minimum, including 1 digit and 1 uppercase.
theme.auth.register.terms: I accept the terms of use and the privacy policy.
theme.auth.register.error.email-taken: This email is already associated with an account. Sign in or use another address.
theme.auth.register.error.email-format: The email must contain an @ and a domain.
theme.auth.register.error.password-weak: Password too weak. Add digits and uppercase letters.
theme.auth.register.error.terms-required: You must accept the terms to continue.
theme.auth.register.success.title: Email sent.
theme.auth.register.success.body: Check your inbox (and spam) to finalize your registration.
theme.auth.register.story.title: Auth-Register — registration pattern
theme.auth.register.story.subtitle: Registration pattern (Ring 3 S3.1) — composes _auth-shell + Field + Input + Button + Checkbox + Alert + Empty (terminal success state). Pure server-rendered. 0 controller in S3.1. Strength meter PLACEHOLDER; full meter ships S3.1b via pattern:Auth-PasswordStrength.
theme.auth.register.story.section.email-: Email already taken (inline error + login suggestion)
theme.auth.register.story.explainer.defa: Initial state: 3 empty fields (name + email + password), unchecked terms, primary submit enabled. autocomplete=name + email + new-password let password managers propose a strong password on generation.
theme.auth.register.story.explainer.emai: Inline error: This email is already associated with an account. The error PROPOSES the action 'Sign in' inline — the user has a direct path to the right flow. Pattern <explanation>. <proposed action>. (cf 12-microcopy §Errors).
theme.auth.register.story.explainer.term: Terms checkbox required (HTML5 required + aria-required + aria-invalid). You must accept to continue. Submit blocks until terms is checked + form re-validated server-side. The checkbox row switches to the invalid variant (red border).
theme.auth.register.story.explainer.load: Loading submit: Button data-loading=true. All Inputs stay visible (the user sees what they typed). The browser prevents double-submit. aria-busy=true → SR announces 'busy'.
theme.auth.register.story.explainer.succ: Empty terminal state replaces the entire form. Icon mail + h2 'Email sent.' + inviting body + secondary CTA 'Resend the email' (if server exposes the resend route). Empty pattern <role=region aria-labelledby> — terminal, not transient (cf 13-a11y §Why no role=status).
theme.auth.forgot-password.title: Forgot password
theme.auth.forgot-password.subtitle: Enter your email; we'll send a link.
theme.auth.forgot-password.error.email-f: The email must contain an @ and a domain.
theme.auth.forgot-password.error.rate-li: Too many requests. Try again in {minutes} minutes.
theme.auth.forgot-password.error.network: Something went wrong on our end. Try again in a moment.
theme.auth.forgot-password.success.title: Email sent.
theme.auth.forgot-password.success.body: If the email matches an account, you'll receive a link in a few minutes.
theme.auth.forgot-password.story.title: Auth-ForgotPassword — reset-request pattern
theme.auth.forgot-password.story.subtitl: Password reset-request pattern (Ring 3 S3.1). Composes _auth-shell + Field + Input + Button + Alert + Empty. Anti-enumeration: the success body never confirms email existence (Stripe / Vercel / Notion pattern).
theme.auth.forgot-password.story.explain: Initial state: empty email, primary submit enabled. The footer exposes 'Back to sign in' (with leading arrow-left icon auto-flipped in RTL).
Loading submit: Button data-loading=true. The email is visually preserved (the user sees what they typed). aria-busy=true.
Rate-limited: Alert variant=warning + submit aria-disabled. The minutes count is consumer-interpolated via t() params. Live countdown deferred Ring 3+.

Empty terminal state replaces the form. The body DOES NOT confirm whether the email exists — anti-enumeration pattern (Stripe / Vercel / Notion). If the email matches an account, you'll receive a link... Optional Resend CTA for spam folder cases.
theme.auth.reset-password.title: New password
theme.auth.reset-password.subtitle: Pick a password you've never used elsewhere.
theme.auth.reset-password.password-help: 8 characters minimum, including 1 digit and 1 uppercase.
