This reset link is no longer valid. Request a new link to continue.
theme.auth.reset-password.token-used.bod: You've already reset your password with this link. Sign in with your new password.
theme.auth.reset-password.story.title: Auth-ResetPassword — reset pattern
theme.auth.reset-password.story.subtitle: Token-bound reset pattern (Ring 3 S3.1). Composes _auth-shell + Field × 2 + Button + Alert + Empty. Strength meter PLACEHOLDER in S3.1; full meter ships S3.1b via pattern:Auth-PasswordStrength + lazy-loaded zxcvbn (OQ-50).
Token already used (Empty terminal + login CTA)
theme.auth.reset-password.story.explaine: Initial state: valid token, 2 password fields with policy help above the field. autocomplete=new-password lets the password manager propose / generate a strong password.

Inline mismatch error on the confirm Field. The user retypes — the policy help stays above the new-password field. Pattern <explanation>. <implicit action: retype>. (consumer JS could fire mismatch on blur — defer Ring 3+).
Inline weak error on the new-password Field. The policy help stays visible ABOVE the error (Field's __help + __error are 2 distinct paragraphs). The user understands the rule + sees what's missing.

Empty terminal state: alert-circle icon + 'Link expired.' + body + primary CTA 'Request a new link' → /password/forgot. The back-to-login footer is HIDDEN (the user doesn't need to return to login from here — Request a new link IS the path).

Empty terminal state: info icon + 'Link already used.' + body + primary CTA 'Sign in' → /login. Typical case: the user already used the link (reloads the email's URL after a successful reset). The back-to-login footer is also hidden.
theme.auth.social-buttons.story.title: Auth-SocialButtons — OAuth providers block
theme.auth.social-buttons.story.subtitle: Composable block (NOT _auth-shell) — drops into Auth-Login/Register/SSO or standalone. Consumer ships socialProviders: [{name, label, icon (raw SVG), action}] array. OQ-52: no ADR-0016 — Lucide doesn't ship brand marks, the kit stays icon-agnostic for OAuth.
theme.auth.social-buttons.story.explaine: Canonical pattern: 'Or continue with' heading + 4 vertical providers. The SVGs are geometric placeholders (circle / square / triangle / 2×2 grid) — deliberately NOT brand-accurate to signal that the consumer ships their own icons via the `icon` prop (Simple Icons / brand assets).
Horizontal orientation: flex row + flex-wrap. Below 30rem viewport, buttons wrap to 2-per-row automatically. Each button keeps min-inline-size 12rem for readability.

Single full-width provider (1 entry in socialProviders). Typical pattern for Auth-SSO continuation: 'Continue with your organization' redirects to the single SSO provider. heading: null for a bare button list.
heading: null hides the 'Or continue with' divider + its lines. Pattern for standalone SocialButtons blocks OR when the parent context already provides its own textual intro.

Canonical composition: SocialButtons drops INSIDE Auth-Login's Card body, BELOW the submit button. The user has 2 paths: password (above) OR social (below). The 'Or continue with' heading acts as a visual divider between the 2 paths.
+30% FR expansion test: long labels (10-15 extra chars). The button grows in inline-size with the label. min-inline-size 12rem (horizontal) holds.
theme.auth.social-buttons.story.sample.l: Continue with my Microsoft Corporate account
Continue with my GitHub Enterprise organization
theme.auth.account-locked.title: Account locked.
theme.auth.account-locked.body: Too many attempts. Contact support to unlock.
theme.auth.account-locked.story.title: Auth-AccountLocked — terminal graduation of rate-limit
theme.auth.account-locked.story.subtitle: Terminal page after N failed attempts (beyond Auth-Login's rateLimitedNotice threshold). Composes _auth-shell (title null — Empty owns h1) + Empty (lg, h1, icon=alert-triangle substitut OQ-57b) + Button × 1-2.
theme.auth.account-locked.story.explaine: Canonical pattern: alert-triangle icon (warning hue) + h1 'Account locked.' + body + primary CTA 'Contact support' (mailto: by default). Mirrors Error-RateLimited's icon treatment — same OQ-57b substitut path because the visual signal is identical (warning, blocked).

With backToLoginHref set, a secondary ghost CTA 'Back to sign in' (with arrow-left iconLeading) offers an escape path to /login. Useful pattern when the lock isn't absolutely terminal (e.g. 24h auto-unlock — the user can return to try later).
theme.auth.email-verification.title: Check your email.
theme.auth.email-verification.body: Email sent. Check your inbox (and spam).
theme.auth.email-verification.story.subt: Terminal page after registration: consumer's auth back-end sends a verification email + redirects the user here. Composes _auth-shell (title null — Empty owns h1) + Empty (lg, h1, icon=check substitut — no mail in curated 30-set) + Button × 1-2 (Resend + optional Back-to-login).
theme.auth.email-verification.story.expl: Canonical pattern: check icon (success hue) + h1 'Check your email.' + body verbatim 12-microcopy §Auth 'Email sent' + secondary Resend CTA. The check icon conveys 'action complete server-side' — substitut for the missing mail/envelope in the curated 30-set (pre-existing S3.1 bug with icon: 'mail' in Auth-Register/ForgotPassword Twig).
With backToLoginHref set, a secondary ghost CTA 'Back to sign in' (with arrow-left iconLeading) offers an escape path. Useful when the user wants to switch accounts or returns via an expired link.
theme.auth.magic-link.title: Link sent.
theme.auth.magic-link.body: Click the link in the email to sign in. Link valid for {expirationMinutes} minutes.
theme.auth.magic-link.story.title: Auth-MagicLink — passwordless sign-in confirmation
theme.auth.magic-link.story.subtitle: Terminal page after a magic link is sent (passwordless flow). Structural twin of Auth-EmailVerification — same shell + Empty + Resend composition, different microcopy. The check icon is tinted --color-primary (vs success for EmailVerification) for visual differentiation.
theme.auth.magic-link.story.explainer.de: Canonical pattern: check icon (primary tint) + h1 'Link sent.' + body with {expirationMinutes}=15 placeholder + Resend CTA. The placeholder is consumer-interpolated via t() params (mirrors Auth-Login rate-limited {minutes}).
theme.auth.magic-link.story.explainer.wi: With backToLoginHref + custom expirationMinutes (30 min): demonstrates the consumer body-interpolation override + the escape path. Pattern for flows with longer expiration policies (B2B / enterprise) or when the user wants to switch to password sign-in.
theme.auth.sso.title: Sign in with your organization
theme.auth.sso.subtitle: Enter your work email — we'll redirect you to your identity provider.
theme.auth.sso.error.no-config: No SSO configuration found for this domain. *Sign in with password*?
theme.auth.sso.story.title: Auth-SSO — enterprise SSO entry
theme.auth.sso.story.subtitle: Enterprise pattern: organization email → consumer's back-end inspects the domain → redirects to the appropriate IDP (Okta / Auth0 / Azure AD / Google Workspace…). Pure server-rendered form (OQ-55); 0 Stimulus controller. Mirrors Auth-Login structure but single-field.
theme.auth.sso.story.explainer.default: Empty form: work email placeholder + 'Continue' submit. autocomplete=email enables password-manager autofill. Tab order: email → submit → [footer link if present].
theme.auth.sso.story.explainer.no-config: The user entered a personal email (gmail) → the consumer back-end finds no SSO config for gmail.com → top-of-form danger Alert proposes the fallback 'Sign in with password'. Pattern <explanation>. <proposed action>. — anti-pattern would be just 'SSO Error'.
theme.auth.sso.story.explainer.loading: data-loading=true on the form + spinner-replaces-iconLeading on submit (width-stable per Button.md). aria-busy=true announces to SR. Native HTML5 prevents double-submit.
theme.auth.sso.story.explainer.fallback: With passwordLoginHref set, the 'No SSO? Sign in with password' footer link offers an escape path to Auth-Login. Useful pattern for apps that support both paths (enterprise SSO + public password).
theme.auth.otp.title: 6-digit code
theme.auth.otp.subtitle: We sent a code to your email.
theme.auth.otp.label: Verification code
theme.auth.otp.error.code-invalid: Code incorrect. *Try again* or *resend the code*.
theme.auth.otp.story.title: Auth-OTP — 6-digit code verification
theme.auth.otp.story.subtitle: Composes _auth-shell + InputOTP (Ring 2 — paste auto-distribute + auto-tab + iOS SMS auto-fill) + Button × 1-3 + Alert. 0 new controller (InputOTP S2.7 handles all the mechanics). Email-fallback opt-in mini-OQ S3.1b — ships 'Receive by email' link alongside Resend when consumer offers both SMS+email paths.
theme.auth.otp.story.section.email-fallb: With email-fallback link (Resend + Receive by email)
theme.auth.otp.story.explainer.default: Empty 6-cell cluster + 'Didn't get it? Resend code' link + 'Verify' submit. Tab order: first cell → cycle cluster → resend → submit. iOS SMS auto-fill via autocomplete='one-time-code' on first cell. Paste 6 digits anywhere → auto-distribute.
theme.auth.otp.story.explainer.error: The user entered a code (123456 via otpValue SSR pre-fill) that doesn't match → top-of-form danger Alert 'Code incorrect.' with inline link 'resend the code'. Pattern <explanation>. <proposed action>. ; both recovery paths (retry + resend) offered in the message.
theme.auth.otp.story.explainer.email-fal: With emailFallbackHref set, the resend row shows the second 'Receive by email' link alongside 'Resend code'. Useful pattern when consumer defaults to SMS-OTP but offers email-OTP fallback (typical case: user without SMS reception).
theme.auth.otp.story.explainer.length-4: otpLength=4 → 4-cell PIN-style cluster. Same controller (input-otp), same auto-tab + paste, just 4 cells. Useful for PIN-only apps or short OTPs (legacy systems). Alphanumeric backup codes (AWS-style ABC1-DEF2-GHI3) are NOT supported — InputOTP is digit-only by doctrine.
theme.auth.password-strength.help: 8 characters minimum, including 1 digit and 1 uppercase.
theme.auth.password-strength.story.title: Auth-PasswordStrength — live meter with zxcvbn lazy
theme.auth.password-strength.story.subti: 1st Ring 3 controller (password-strength). Composable mini-block — drops INSIDE Auth-Register / Auth-ResetPassword form bodies. zxcvbn-ts v3.x lazy-loaded on first keystroke per ADR-0018 (~30 kB chunk, mirrors ADR-0011/0012/0013 verbatim). 3-tier score map: 0-1 danger, 2 warning, 3-4 success.
theme.auth.password-strength.story.secti: Weak (Password1! — dictionary match catches naive pattern)
Very strong (correct horse battery staple — XKCD canonical)
With server error (meter unchanged, Field shows error)
theme.auth.password-strength.story.expla: Initial state: Progress value=0/variant=danger + 'Enter a password.' hint. No zxcvbn fetch (lazy on first keystroke — saves chunk for bounce-out paths). Controller dispatches password-strength:mount on connect().

'Password1!' is the canonical anti-pattern: 3 character classes + 9 chars → naive scoring awards score=4 (very strong). zxcvbn correctly detects the 'password' dictionary match → score=1 (weak). This is THE UX differentiator vs home-grown weighted formula (OQ-50 → zxcvbn chosen for this reason).

'Tr0ub4dor' has character classes + L33t-speak that zxcvbn maps to the 'troubadour' dictionary → score=2 (medium, but triggers valid=true if minScore=2). Neutral tutoiement in the tier label 'Medium' — no blame.

'correct horse battery staple' — the canonical XKCD passphrase. zxcvbn computes entropy via dictionary + length → score=4 (very strong). iOS/Android password managers typically suggest this style for autocomplete=new-password.

Server error (e.g. 'Email already in use') shows in the Field's __error slot with aria-invalid=true. The meter stays at its last computed state (here score=2 warning) — the password itself isn't invalid, just the associated email.
theme.auth.password-strength.story.sampl: This email is already associated with an account. *Sign in* or use a different address.
theme.auth.session-timeout-modal.title: Inactivity detected
theme.auth.session-timeout-modal.body-be: For your security, we'll sign you out in
theme.auth.session-timeout-modal.story.s: 2nd Ring 3 controller (session-timeout-countdown). Cross-controller compose 'dialog session-timeout-countdown' — Dialog (Ring 2) owns modal mechanics + focus trap + Esc/backdrop policy; session-timeout-countdown owns the live countdown + warning threshold (default 30s) + expired event. closeOnEscape + closeOnBackdropClick BOTH false (destructive-confirm doctrine per §Confirmations).
Long countdown (3 min — no urgency yet)
theme.auth.session-timeout-modal.story.e: Modal open at render — 1 min remaining. aria-live='polite' on the countdown span (SR re-announces at minute boundary). Tab cycle: Stay signed in (autofocus) → Sign out. Esc + backdrop click BOTH ignored (destructive-confirm doctrine).

20s remaining — under threshold (30s default). Controller dispatches session-timeout:warning ONCE + re-stamps aria-live='assertive' on the countdown (OQ-61). SR users hear the urgency. Visual stays subdued (no colour shift in v1 — opt-in via data-state='urgent' deferred Ring 3+).

3 min remaining. Modal could open at this stage if consumer's policy is 'warn 5 min before expiry'. The countdown renders the 'minutes-other' plural variant via Intl.PluralRules ('3 minutes', not '3 minute').
theme.error-shell.story.title: _error-shell — Errors patterns layout shell
theme.error-shell.story.subtitle: Sub-template shared by the 10 Error-* patterns (Ring 3 S3.1c). Centered viewport-min-height + 2 variants (default icon-feel / hero brand-feel) + safe-area-inset. Mirrors _auth-shell doctrine verbatim.
theme.error-shell.story.explainer.defaul: Variant 'default': Empty primitive with a Lucide icon (substitute per OQ-57b) + h1 + body + actions. Shared pattern for the 7 error-feel patterns (401/403/404/500/503/BrowserUnsupported/RateLimited).
theme.error-shell.story.explainer.hero: Variant 'hero': Empty primitive with a custom inline SVG (consumer-overridable via the illustration block) + h1 + body + actions. Shared pattern for the 3 brand-feel patterns (Maintenance / Offline / ComingSoon).
theme.error-shell.story.explainer.with-f: The shell exposes an optional footer for secondary links (e.g. 'Check status', 'Request help'). Hidden by default when the footer block isn't defined.
theme.error-shell.story.sample.error-fee: Composition Empty primitive + Lucide icon. Doctrine for the 7 patterns 401/403/404/500/503/BrowserUnsupported/RateLimited.
theme.error-shell.story.sample.brand-fee: Composition Empty primitive + custom inline SVG hero. Doctrine for the 3 patterns Maintenance / Offline / ComingSoon.
theme.error-shell.story.sample.with-foot: The footer hosts a secondary link (status page, access request, etc.). Optional — hidden when block undefined.

theme.error.500.story.explainer.default: Canonical pattern: server error. Primary Try again + Secondary Back to home. Microcopy declines the 'we' (service responsibility, not user).
theme.error.500.story.explainer.primary-: Variant without secondary — for contexts where back-to-home doesn't make sense (e.g. inside an iframe).

theme.lang-switcher.story.title: LangSwitcher — locale picker
theme.lang-switcher.story.subtitle: Composes DropdownMenu Ring 2 (Popover + Item) with consumer-provided locales array. The lang-switcher controller dispatches lang-switcher:change + persists to localStorage. Consumer wires page reload OR client-side i18n flip via the event.
theme.lang-switcher.story.section.five: 5 languages (incl. AR for RTL stress test)
theme.lang-switcher.story.explainer.defa: Trigger = active locale code (FR) + chevron-down. Click opens DropdownMenu ; click on an option dispatches lang-switcher:change + persists to theme.locale in localStorage.
theme.lang-switcher.story.explainer.with: Optional flag emojis via the locales[].flag prop. Pure semantic decoration (aria-hidden) — the trigger aria-label stays 'Change language'.
theme.lang-switcher.story.explainer.five: 5 locales including Arabic to validate the dropdown's RTL rendering (logical placement via Popover Ring 2). EN active at story start.
theme.density-switcher.story.title: DensitySwitcher — interface density
theme.density-switcher.story.subtitle: Composes ToggleGroup Ring 1 (single mode mutex) inline + Toggle Ring 1. The density-switcher controller flips data-density on the target element (documentElement / body / self) + persists to localStorage + dispatches density-switcher:change.
theme.density-switcher.story.explainer.d: Initial state. The 'Comfortable' toggle has aria-pressed=true. data-density='comfortable' is applied to the wrap (target='self' for story isolation).
theme.density-switcher.story.explainer.c: Cozy active: row ~40 px, reduced padding. A compromise between comfort and information density.
Compact active: row ~32 px, minimal padding. Max information density — reserved for advanced tables.
theme.theme-switcher.story.title: ThemeSwitcher — light / dark / system
theme.theme-switcher.story.subtitle: Composes ToggleGroup Ring 1 (single mode mutex) inline + Toggle Ring 1. The theme-switcher controller flips data-theme on the target element + persists the USER'S CHOICE (light | dark | system) — not the resolved theme — to preserve the 'system' preference across sessions. In system mode, listens to prefers-color-scheme + re-dispatches on OS-level theme changes.
theme.theme-switcher.story.explainer.sys: The 'System' toggle is aria-pressed=true. The controller resolves the effective theme via window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)') and listens for OS-level changes (macOS auto-dark at sunset, etc.). The 'system' choice is persisted in localStorage — not the resolved theme.
theme.theme-switcher.story.explainer.lig: 'Light' toggle pressed. The controller removes the data-theme attribute from the target (default styles are light). No prefers-color-scheme listener.
theme.theme-switcher.story.explainer.dar: 'Dark' toggle pressed. The controller sets data-theme='dark' on the target. No prefers-color-scheme listener.

theme.danger-zone.story.subtitle: Composes Card variant=elevated + Button variant=destructive. 2 variants : inline (typed-confirmation with danger-zone controller) + modal (composes AlertDialog Ring 2 — pure composition, no controller). theme.danger-zone.story.explainer.inline: Initial state. Empty input. Button disabled + aria-disabled='true'. Border --color-danger-soft (subtle).
Input pre-filled with 'SUPPRIMER' (exact match). Button enabled. Border --color-danger (full). The controller dispatched danger-zone:match-change with matches=true.
theme.danger-zone.story.explainer.modal: Card + destructive Button only (no inline input). Click → consumer wires data-action='click->dialog#open' on a separately-rendered AlertDialog Ring 2. The story does not render the AlertDialog (visual isolation) — consumer composes at page level.

theme.keyboard-shortcuts-overlay.title: Keyboard shortcuts
Press Esc or click outside to close.
theme.keyboard-shortcuts-overlay.story.s: Pure composition Dialog Ring 2 (size=lg) + Kbd Ring 1. NO controller — the trigger (typically `?` keypress) is wired by the consumer's app-level controller to avoid global side-effects. The kit ships modal mechanics + Kbd display ; the consumer ships the keybind policy.
theme.keyboard-shortcuts-overlay.story.e: Dialog size=lg rendered open inline for visual baseline capture. 3 sections : Navigation (Cmd+K, G+I sequence, G+P sequence, Esc) + Editing (Cmd+S, Cmd+Z, Cmd+⇧+Z) + Search (/, ?). In production, the consumer wires a keydown.? listener at app-level + calls dialog#open via getControllerForElementAndIdentifier.

theme.search-results.empty.body: No results for « %s ». Try another keyword or broaden your search.
theme.search-results.story.title: Search-Results — search results list
theme.search-results.story.subtitle: Pure composition Item Ring 1 list. NO controller. The consumer provides the results array via Twig ; each row is an Item (typically `as: 'a'` with href to the detail page). Header displays the count via Intl.PluralRules-aware microcopy (consumer pre-resolves the count phrase). Empty state composed via Empty Ring 1 when results.length === 0.
theme.search-results.story.explainer.def: 4 labels-only results with iconLeading + chevron-right trailing. Each row is an `<a href>` Item Ring 1 — native focus + Enter activates. Header shows the count + the query.
theme.search-results.story.explainer.wit: Results with description (2-line rows). Common pattern for search-results with snippet/context. Item Ring 1 natively supports the description slot.
theme.search-results.story.explainer.mix: Mix of link + button per result (navigation vs action). Item Ring 1 supports `as: 'a' | 'button' | 'div'` — visually identical, semantically distinct.
theme.search-results.story.explainer.emp: Empty state composed via Empty Ring 1 with search icon + invite microcopy. Tone follows 12-microcopy-tone.md §Empty states : invite, don't blame.

theme.search-results.story.sample.settin: Avatar, name, email, time zone, password

theme.search-faceted-filters.story.title: Search-FacetedFilters — facet filter panel
theme.search-faceted-filters.story.subti: Composes Checkbox Ring 1 (multi-select facets) + Slider Ring 1 (range facets) + optional Popover Ring 2 (mobile filter chips). Controller faceted-filters (16th Ring 3, thin) aggregates input changes into a state object + dispatches faceted-filters:change. Markup-agnostic : reads every <input> via FormData-like aggregation, supports checkbox/radio/range/number/text. Empty values are omitted from state — consumer detects 'unchanged' implicitly.
theme.search-faceted-filters.story.secti: Default (3 groups : categories + brand + max price)
With initial selections (2 categories + 1 brand + price 250)
theme.search-faceted-filters.story.expla: 3 fieldset groups : Categories (4 multi-select checkboxes) + Brand (3 checkboxes) + Max Price (range 0-1000). The controller aggregates each change/input event and dispatches faceted-filters:change with the full state object.
Demo initial state : 2 categories checked (electronics + clothing), 1 brand (samsung), price 250. The Clear button resets all + dispatches faceted-filters:cleared.
Toggle the checkboxes + slide the range — events appear live in the log below. Event detail contains { state, changed: { name, value, type } }.
theme.search-command-palette.label: Global search
theme.search-command-palette.story.title: Search-CommandPalette — ⌘K global search palette
theme.search-command-palette.story.subti: Pure composition Command Ring 2 (Dialog + Combobox + Kbd + transitive lazy Floating UI via Popover). NO new controller, NO new lib (Fuse.js stays candidate per OQ-NEW sealed S3.3b-2 opening). The wrapper adds search-focused microcopy defaults + an optional launcher button (typically in the app header) + a marker class for consumer-differentiated styling. The global ⌘K hotkey + window.themeCommand.register({...}) API are inherited from Command Ring 2.
theme.search-command-palette.story.expla: Ghost button with search icon + placeholder label + ⌘K Kbd hint. Consumer typically places it in the global app header. Click → window.themeCommand.open() — the global ⌘K hotkey works in parallel.

Palette pre-rendered inline for visual baseline. In production, the palette opens via the ⌘K hotkey (window-scoped) OR the launcher button OR window.themeCommand.open(). 3 grouped option sections (Navigation + Docs + Actions).
The command:open / command:close / command:execute events appear live in the log below. detail.id + detail.label + detail.group available in command:execute.
💡 Tip : call `window.themeCommand.open()` from anywhere in your app to open the palette programmatically (without hotkey).

theme.rgpd-data-export.description: Get a copy of your personal data in a machine-readable format.
theme.rgpd-data-export.legal-note: Under GDPR article 20 (right to data portability), you can request a copy of your data. The export is processed asynchronously — you'll receive an email with the download link.
theme.rgpd-data-export.story.title: RGPD-DataExport — GDPR data portability affordance
theme.rgpd-data-export.story.subtitle: Pure composition Card Ring 1 (variant=elevated) + Button Ring 1. NO controller — the consumer wires the API call + state machine (consumer sets `data-rgpd-data-export-state` on the Card wrap to flip between idle / loading / success / error). The kit ships the 4 state CSS hooks + the microcopy label per state. GDPR article 20 (right to data portability).
theme.rgpd-data-export.story.explainer.i: Initial state. The user hasn't requested an export yet. Click on the button → consumer dispatches the API call + flips data-state to loading.
theme.rgpd-data-export.story.explainer.l: The consumer has dispatched the API call. The button is disabled + the label flips to 'Export in progress…' + option icon (Lucide). The consumer polls OR waits for an SSE/Mercure notification to flip to success/error.
theme.rgpd-data-export.story.explainer.s: Export is ready. The label flips to 'Download ready' + arrow-right icon + success tone (token --color-success-bg). The consumer wires the download click on the button (file href or window.open).
theme.rgpd-data-export.story.explainer.e: Export failed (timeout, server error, etc.). The label flips to 'Error — retry' + alert-triangle icon + danger tone. Click → consumer re-dispatches the API call (flips back to loading).
theme.rgpd-cookie-banner.title: Cookies & privacy
theme.rgpd-cookie-banner.legal-summary: We use cookies to run this site, measure traffic and offer you relevant content. You can accept all, reject all, or customize your choice.
theme.rgpd-cookie-banner.legal-detail: Learn more in our privacy policy and our cookies page.
theme.rgpd-cookie-banner.story.title: RGPD-CookieBanner — cookie consent banner
theme.rgpd-cookie-banner.story.subtitle: Composes Dialog Ring 2 (closeOnEscape=false + closeOnBackdropClick=false per GDPR legal commit). Controller cookie-banner (17th Ring 3, thin) — defensive Both document.cookie + localStorage per OQ-NEW sealed S3.3b-2 opening. 11th cross-controller compose. 3 CTAs : Accept all (dispatches accept) / Reject all (dispatches reject) / Customize (dispatches customize — consumer wires opening of RGPD-PreferencesCenter). saveCustom(obj) public method called by consumer after PreferencesCenter save.
theme.rgpd-cookie-banner.story.explainer: Modal dialog banner with legal-summary + legal-detail + 3 CTAs. closeOnEscape=false + closeOnBackdropClick=false → user MUST commit to a button. Click Accept / Reject persists Both cookie + localStorage + dispatches event + closes dialog. Click Customize dispatches customize (consumer opens PreferencesCenter).

Variant with inline preview of the 3 categories (Essential always active + Analytics optional + Marketing optional). More informative without requiring the user to open PreferencesCenter for the majority case.
Click on the 3 CTAs — events appear live. cookie-banner:accept / reject / customize / save-custom (the latter called via saveCustom(obj) public method).
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.title: My cookie preferences
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.descriptio: Customize your consent by category. Essential cookies are always active (required for the site to function). Other categories are optional — you can enable or disable them at any time.
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.footer-inf: Your preferences are saved for 180 days. You can modify them at any time from this page.
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.category.e: Strictly necessary for the site to function (authentication, cart, security). Cannot be disabled.
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.category.a: Help us understand how you use the site (pages visited, time spent, journey). Anonymized data — cannot identify you.
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.category.m: Allow us to show you personalized ads on other sites + measure their effectiveness. Data shared with our advertising partners.
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.category.f: Remember your preferences (language, theme, density). Improve your user experience without collecting identifying data.
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.story.subt: Composes Dialog Ring 2 size=lg + Switch Ring 1 per category. Controller preferences-center (18th Ring 3, thin) tracks multi-category state + dirty detection + explicit Save button per OQ-NEW sealed (GDPR legal intentional commit, NOT instant-save). 12th cross-controller compose. The Essential category is always-on (disabled=true) — strictly necessary. Save dispatches preferences-center:save with the full state object ; consumer wires it to cookie-banner.saveCustom(state) to persist via the defensive Both cookie + localStorage write.
theme.rgpd-preferences-center.story.expl: 4 standard categories : Essential (disabled, always-on) + Analytics (off by default) + Marketing (off by default) + Functional (on by default). User toggles then Save → dispatches preferences-center:save. Cancel resets to initial state.

Toggle Analytics ON → preferences-center:dirty true. Toggle Analytics OFF (back to initial) → preferences-center:dirty false. Click Save → preferences-center:save + state becomes the new baseline (dirty=false after save).
